title: Power and Sample Size for Cochran-Armitage Trend Test for Ordered Multi-Sample Binomial Response — getDesignOrderedBinom
text: S3 class designOrderedBinom object following components: power: power reject null hypothesis. alpha: two-sided significance level. n: maximum number subjects. ngroups: number treatment groups. pi: response probabilities treatment groups. w: scores assigned treatment groups. trendstat: Cochran-Armitage trend test statistic. allocationRatioPlanned: Allocation ratio treatment groups. rounding: Whether round sample size.

title: Power and Sample Size for Cochran-Armitage Trend Test for Ordered Multi-Sample Binomial Response — getDesignOrderedBinom
text: ordered multi-sample binomial response design used test whether response probabilities differ across multiple treatment groups. null hypothesis response probabilities equal across treatment groups, alternative hypothesis response probabilities ordered, .e. response probability increases treatment group index. Cochran-Armitage trend test used test hypothesis. test effectively regresses response probabilities treatment group scores, test whether slope regression line significantly different zero. trend parameter defined $$\theta = \sum_{g=1}^{G} r_g (w_g - \bar{w}) \pi_g$$ \(G\) number treatment groups, \(r_g\) randomization probability treatment group \(g\), \(w_g\) score assigned treatment group \(g\), \(\pi_g\) response probability treatment group \(g\), \(\bar{w} = \sum_{g=1}^{G} r_g w_g\) weighted average score across treatment groups. Since \(\hat{\theta}\) linear combination estimated response probabilities, variance given $$Var(\hat{\theta}) = \frac{1}{n}\sum_{g=1}^{G} r_g (w_g - \bar{w})^2 \pi_g(1-\pi_g)$$ \(n\) total sample size. sample size chosen power reject null hypothesis least \(1-\beta\) given significance level \(\alpha\).

title: Group Sequential Design for McNemar's Test for Paired Proportions — getDesignPairedPropMcNemar
text: Consider group sequential design McNemar's test paired proportions. table shows joint probabilities cell (\(\pi_{ij}\) \(\) control group \(j\) experimental group), marginal totals. parameters \(\pi_{01}\) \(\pi_{10}\) discordant pairs (.e., \(\pi_{01} + \pi_{10} = \xi\)) risk difference \(\pi_{01} - \pi_{10} = \delta\). parameter \(\pi_t\) proportion experimental group response, \(\pi_c\) proportion control group response. parameter interest $$\theta = \pi_t - \pi_c = \pi_{01} - \pi_{10} = \delta$$ variance \(\hat{\theta}\) can obtained multinomial distribution follows: $$Var(\hat{\theta}) = \frac{1}{n} \{ \pi_{01}(1-\pi_{01}) + \pi_{10}(1-\pi_{10}) + 2\pi_{01}\pi_{10} \}$$ can simplified $$Var(\hat{\theta}) = \frac{1}{n} (\xi - \delta^2)$$ , \(n\) total number treatment pairs. unconditional variance, used overall design.

title: Power and Sample Size for Repeated-Measures ANOVA — getDesignRepeatedANOVA
text: Let \(y_{ij}\) denote measurement treatment condition \(j (j=1,\ldots,k)\) subject \((=1,\ldots,n)\). $$y_{ij} = \alpha + \beta_j + b_i + e_{ij}$$ \(b_i\) denotes subject random effect, \(b_i \sim N(0, \sigma_b^2)\) \(e_{ij} \sim N(0, \sigma_e^2)\) denotes within-subject residual. set \(\beta_k = 0\), \(\alpha\) mean last treatment (control), \(\beta_j\) difference means \(j\)th treatment control \(j=1,\ldots,k-1\). repeated measures compound symmetry covariance structure. Let \(\sigma^2 = \sigma_b^2 + \sigma_e^2\), \(\rho = \frac{\sigma_b^2}{\sigma_b^2 + \sigma_e^2}\). \(Var(y_i) = \sigma^2 \{(1-\rho) I_k + \rho 1_k 1_k^T\}\). Let \(X_i\) denote design matrix subject \(\). Let \(\theta = (\alpha, \beta_1, \ldots, \beta_{k-1})^T\). follows $$Var(\hat{\theta}) = \left(\sum_{=1}^{n} X_i^T V_i^{-1} X_i\right)^{-1}.$$ can shown $$Var(\hat{\beta}) = \frac{\sigma^2 (1-\rho)}{n} (I_{k-1} + 1_{k-1} 1_{k-1}^T).$$ follows \(\hat{\beta}^T \hat{V}_{\hat{\beta}}^{-1} \hat{\beta} \sim F_{k-1,(n-1)(k-1), \lambda}\) noncentrality parameter \(F\) distribution $$\lambda = \beta^T V_{\hat{\beta}}^{-1} \beta = \frac{n \sum_{j=1}^{k} (\mu_j - \bar{\mu})^2}{\sigma^2(1-\rho)}.$$

text: Consider group sequential design two-sample risk difference. parameter interest $$\theta = \pi_1 - \pi_2$$ \(\pi_1\) response probability active treatment group \(\pi_2\) response probability control group. variance \(\hat{\theta}\) can obtained binomial distributions follows: $$Var(\hat{\theta}) = \frac{1}{n} \{ \frac{\pi_1(1-\pi_1)}{r} + \frac{\pi_2(1-\pi_2)}{1-r} \}$$ \(n\) total number subjects \(r\) randomization probability active treatment group. nullVariance = TRUE, variance computed null hypothesis. case, values \(\pi_1\) \(\pi_2\) variance formula replaced restricted maximum likelihood counterparts, subject constraint $$\pi_1 - \pi_2 = \theta_0$$

title: Group Sequential Design for Two-Sample Risk Ratio — getDesignRiskRatio
text: S3 class designRiskRatio object three components: overallResults: data frame containing following variables: overallReject: overall rejection probability. alpha: overall significance level. attainedAlpha: attained significance level, different overall significance level presence futility stopping. kMax: number stages. theta: parameter value. information: maximum information. expectedInformationH1: expected information H1. expectedInformationH0: expected information H0. drift: drift parameter, equal theta*sqrt(information). inflationFactor: inflation factor (relative fixed design). numberOfSubjects: maximum number subjects. expectedNumberOfSubjectsH1: expected number subjects H1. expectedNumberOfSubjectsH0: expected number subjects H0. riskRatioH0: risk ratio null hypothesis. pi1: assumed probability active treatment group. pi2: assumed probability control group. byStageResults: data frame containing following variables: informationRates: information rates. efficacyBounds: efficacy boundaries Z-scale. futilityBounds: futility boundaries Z-scale. rejectPerStage: probability efficacy stopping. futilityPerStage: probability futility stopping. cumulativeRejection: cumulative probability efficacy stopping. cumulativeFutility: cumulative probability futility stopping. cumulativeAlphaSpent: cumulative alpha spent. efficacyP: efficacy boundaries p-value scale. futilityP: futility boundaries p-value scale. information: cumulative information. efficacyStopping: Whether allow efficacy stopping. futilityStopping: Whether allow futility stopping. rejectPerStageH0: probability efficacy stopping H0. futilityPerStageH0: probability futility stopping H0. cumulativeRejectionH0: cumulative probability efficacy stopping H0. cumulativeFutilityH0: cumulative probability futility stopping H0. efficacyRiskRatio: efficacy boundaries risk ratio scale. futilityRiskRatio: futility boundaries risk ratio scale. numberOfSubjects: number subjects. settings: list containing following input parameters: typeAlphaSpending: type alpha spending. parameterAlphaSpending: parameter value alpha spending. userAlphaSpending: user defined alpha spending. typeBetaSpending: type beta spending. parameterBetaSpending: parameter value beta spending. userBetaSpending: user defined beta spending. spendingTime: error spending time analysis. varianceRatio: ratio variance H0 variance H1. nullVariance: Whether use variance null empirical variance alternative. allocationRatioPlanned: Allocation ratio active treatment versus control. rounding: Whether round sample size.

title: Group Sequential Design for Two-Sample Risk Ratio — getDesignRiskRatio
text: Consider group sequential design two-sample risk ratio. parameter interest $$\rho = \pi_1 / \pi_2$$ \(\pi_1\) response probability active treatment group \(\pi_2\) response probability control group. statistical inference, parameter often transformed log scale: $$\theta = \log(\rho) = \log(\pi_1) - \log(\pi_2)$$ variance estimator \(\hat{\theta}\) can derived binomial distributions follows: $$Var(\hat{\theta}) = \frac{1}{n} \{ \frac{1-\pi_1}{\pi_1 r} + \frac{1-\pi_2}{\pi_2(1-r)} \}$$ \(n\) total number subjects \(r\) randomization probability active treatment group. nullVariance = TRUE, variance computed null hypothesis. case, values \(\pi_1\) \(\pi_2\) variance formula replaced restricted maximum likelihood counterparts, subject constraint $$\pi_1 / \pi_2 = \rho_0$$

title: Group Sequential Design for Two-Sample Risk Ratio Based on the Farrington-Manning Score Test — getDesignRiskRatioFM
text: Consider group sequential design two-sample risk ratio. parameter interest $$\rho = \pi_1 / \pi_2$$ \(\pi_1\) response probability active treatment group \(\pi_2\) response probability control group. Let \(\rho_0\) denote risk ratio null hypothesis. Farrington-Manning score test statistic constructed $$Z = \frac{\hat{\pi}_1 - \rho_0 \hat{\pi}_2}{ \sqrt{Var(\hat{\pi}_1 - \rho_0 \hat{\pi}_2)}}$$ variance can derived binomial distributions follows: $$Var(\hat{\pi}_1 - \rho_0 \hat{\pi}_2) = \frac{1}{n} \{ \frac{\pi_1(1-\pi_1)}{r} + \frac{\rho_0^2\pi_2(1-\pi_2)}{1-r} \}$$ \(n\) total number subjects \(r\) randomization probability active treatment group. nullVariance = TRUE, variance computed null hypothesis. case, values \(\pi_1\) \(\pi_2\) variance formula replaced restricted maximum likelihood counterparts, subject constraint $$\pi_1 / \pi_2 = \rho_0$$

title: Group Sequential Design for Two-Sample Slope Difference — getDesignSlopeDiff
text: assume simple linear regression form $$y_{g,} = \alpha_g + \beta_g x_{g,} + \epsilon_{g,}$$ treatment group \(g\), \(\epsilon_{g,}\) residual error subject \(\) group \(g\), assumed normally distributed mean 0 standard deviation \(\sigma_\epsilon\). covariate \(x_{g,}\) assumed normally distributed mean 0 standard deviation \(\sigma_x\). Since $$\hat{\beta}_g = \frac{\sum_{=1}^{n_g} (x_{g,}-\bar{x}_g) y_{g,}} {\sum_{=1}^{n_g}(x_{g,}-\bar{x}_g)^2}$$ \(n_g\) sample size group \(g\), follows $$\hat{\beta}_g \sim N(\beta_g, \frac{\sigma_\epsilon^2}{\sum_{=1}^{n_g}(x_{g,}-\bar{x}_g)^2}).$$ slope difference defined $$\hat{\beta}_1 - \hat{\beta}_2$$ \(\hat{\beta}_1\) \(\hat{\beta}_2\) estimated slopes treatment groups 1 2, respectively. Since variance \(\hat{\beta}_g\) $$\frac{\sigma_\epsilon^2}{n_g \sigma_x^2}$$ $$\hat{\beta}_1 - \hat{\beta}_2 \sim N(\beta_1 - \beta_2, (n_1^{-1} + n_2^{-1})\sigma_\epsilon^2/\sigma_x^2)$$ can used calculate power sample size group sequential design.

title: Group Sequential Design for Two-Sample Slope Difference From the MMRM Model — getDesignSlopeDiffMMRM
text: use following random-effects model compare two slopes: $$y_{ij} = \alpha + (\beta + \gamma x_i) t_j + a_i + b_i t_j + e_{ij}$$ \(\alpha\): overall intercept common across treatment groups due randomization \(\beta\): slope control group \(\gamma\): difference slopes active treatment control groups \(x_i\): treatment indicator subject \(\), 1 active treatment 0 control \(t_j\): time point \(j\) repeated measurements, \(t_1 = 0 < t_2 < \ldots < t_k\) \((a_i, b_i)\): random intercept random slope subject \(\), \(Var(a_i) = \sigma_a^2\), \(Var(b_i) = \sigma_b^2\), \(Corr(a_i, b_i) = \rho\) \(e_{ij}\): within-subject residual variance \(\sigma_e^2\) accounting randomization, improve efficiency estimating difference slopes. model also accommodates unequally spaced time points missing data. Specifically, given calendar time \(\tau\) interim final analysis, let \(k\) number scheduled time points including \(\tau\), subject follow-duration fixed follow-designs. Let observed time points \(t_1, t_2, \ldots, t_k\), \(t_1 = 0\) denotes baseline. subject treatment group \(g\) observed data pattern \(j\), design matrix fixed effects \((\alpha, \beta, \gamma)'\) given $$X_{g,j} = (\bm{1}_j, \vec{t}_j, (g=1)\vec{t}_j)$$ \(\bm{1}_j\) \(j\)-vector ones, \(\vec{t}_j = (t_1,\ldots,t_j)'\) column vector observed time points. design matrix random effects \((a_i, b_i)'\) $$Z_j = (\bm{1}_j, \vec{t}_j)$$ variance-covariance matrix random effects $$D = \left(\begin{array}{cc} \sigma_a^2 & \rho \sigma_a \sigma_b \\ \rho \sigma_a \sigma_b & \sigma_b^2 \end{array}\right)$$ Therefore, variance-covariance matrix observed data subject $$V_{j} = Z_j D Z_j' + \sigma_e^2 I_j$$ \(I_j\) \(j\times j\) identity matrix. Let \(\pi_g\) denote proportion subjects group \(g\). information matrix fixed effects $$= nJ$$ $$J = \sum_{g=1}^{2} \pi_g \sum_{j=1}^{k} p_{g,j} X_{g,j}' V_j^{-1} X_{g,j}$$ \(p_{g,j}\) proportion subjects group \(g\) observed data pattern \(j\). variance estimator slope difference \(\hat{\gamma}\) given $$\text{Var}(\hat{\gamma}) = \frac{1}{n} J^{-1}[3,3]$$ can used calculate power sample size group sequential design detect slope difference.

title: Power and Sample Size for Difference in Two-Sample Multinomial Responses — getDesignTwoMultinom
text: two-arm multinomial response design used test whether prevalence category differs two treatment arms. Let \(\pi_{gi}\) denote prevalence category \(\) group \(g\), \(g=1\) treatment group \(g=2\) control group. chi-square test statistic given $$X^2 = \sum_{g=1}^{2} \sum_{=1}^{C} \frac{(n_{gi} - n_{g+} n_{+}/n)^2}{n_{g+} n_{+}/n}$$ \(n_{gi}\) number subjects category \(\) group \(g\), \(n_{g+}\) total number subjects group \(g\), \(n_{+}\) total number subjects category \(\) across groups, \(n\) total sample size. null hypothesis, \(X^2\) follows chi-square distribution \(C-1\) degrees freedom. alternative hypothesis, \(X^2\) follows non-central chi-square distribution non-centrality parameter $$\lambda = n r (1-r) \sum_{=1}^{C} \frac{(\pi_{1i} - \pi_{2i})^2}   {r \pi_{1i} + (1-r)\pi_{2i}}$$ \(r\) randomization probability active treatment. sample size chosen power reject null hypothesis least \(1-\beta\) given significance level \(\alpha\).

title: Power and Sample Size for the Wilcoxon Test for Two-Sample Ordinal Response — getDesignTwoOrdinal
text: two-sample ordinal response design used test whether ordinal response distributions differ two treatment arms. Let \(\pi_{gi}\) denote prevalence category \(\) group \(g\), \(g=1\) represents treatment group \(g=2\) represents control group. parameter interest $$\theta = \sum_{=1}^{C} w_i (\pi_{1i} - \pi_{2i})$$ \(w_i\) midrank score category \(\). Z-test statistic given $$Z = \hat{\theta}/\sqrt{Var(\hat{\theta})}$$ \(\hat{\theta}\) estimate \(\theta\). midrank score \(w_i\) category \(\) calculated : $$w_i = \sum_{j=1}^{} \pi_j - 0.5\pi_i$$ \(\pi_i = r\pi_{1i} + (1-r)\pi_{2i}\) denotes average prevalence category \(\) across groups, \(r\) randomization probability active treatment. understand midrank score, consider \(n\pi_i\) subjects category \(\). midrank score average rank subjects: $$s_i = \frac{1}{n\pi_i} \sum_{j=1}^{n\pi_i} ( n\pi_1 + \cdots + n\pi_{-1} + j)$$ simplifies $$s_i = n\left(\sum_{j=1}^{} \pi_j - 0.5\pi_i\right) + \frac{1}{2}$$ dividing \(n\) ignoring \(\frac{1}{2n}\), obtain midrank score \(w_i\). variance \(\hat{\theta}\) can derived multinomial distributions given $$Var(\hat{\theta}) = \frac{1}{n}\sum_{g=1}^{2} \frac{1}{r_g} \left\{\sum_{=1}^{C} w_i^2\pi_{gi} - \left(\sum_{=1}^{C} w_i\pi_{gi} \right)^2\right\}$$ \(r_g\) randomization probability group \(g\). sample size chosen power reject null hypothesis least \(1-\beta\) given significance level \(\alpha\).

title: Power and Sample Size for the Wilcoxon Test for Two-Sample Ordinal Response — getDesignTwoOrdinal
text: Kaifeng Lu, kaifenglu@gmail.com

text: S3 class designUnorderedBinom object following components: power: power reject null hypothesis. alpha: two-sided significance level. n: maximum number subjects. ngroups: number treatment groups. pi: response probabilities treatment groups. effectsize: effect size chi-square test. allocationRatioPlanned: Allocation ratio treatment groups. rounding: Whether round sample size.

title: Power and Sample Size for Unordered Multi-Sample Binomial Response — getDesignUnorderedBinom
text: multi-sample binomial response design used test whether response probabilities differ among multiple treatment arms. Let \(\pi_{g}\) denote response probability group \(g = 1,\ldots,G\), \(G\) total number treatment groups. chi-square test statistic given $$X^2 = \sum_{g=1}^{G} \sum_{=1}^{2} \frac{(n_{gi} - n_{g+}n_{+}/n)^2}{n_{g+} n_{+}/n}$$ \(n_{gi}\) number subjects category \(\) group \(g\), \(n_{g+}\) total number subjects group \(g\), \(n_{+}\) total number subjects category \(\) across groups, \(n\) total sample size. Let \(r_g\) denote randomization probability group \(g\), define weighted average response probability across groups $$\bar{\pi} = \sum_{g=1}^{G} r_g \pi_g$$ null hypothesis, \(X^2\) follows chi-square distribution \(G-1\) degrees freedom. alternative hypothesis, \(X^2\) follows non-central chi-square distribution non-centrality parameter $$\lambda = n \sum_{g=1}^{G} \frac{r_g (\pi_{g} - \bar{\pi})^2}   {\bar{\pi} (1-\bar{\pi})}$$ sample size chosen power reject null hypothesis least \(1-\beta\) given significance level \(\alpha\).

title: Power and Sample Size for Unordered Multi-Sample Multinomial Response — getDesignUnorderedMultinom
text: multi-sample multinomial response design used test whether response probabilities differ among multiple treatment arms. Let \(\pi_{gi}\) denote response probability category \(= 1,\ldots,C\) group \(g = 1,\ldots,G\), \(G\) total number treatment groups, \(C\) total number categories response variable. chi-square test statistic given $$X^2 = \sum_{g=1}^{G} \sum_{=1}^{C} \frac{(n_{gi} - n_{g+}n_{+}/n)^2}{n_{g+} n_{+}/n}$$ \(n_{gi}\) number subjects category \(\) group \(g\), \(n_{g+}\) total number subjects group \(g\), \(n_{+}\) total number subjects category \(\) across groups, \(n\) total sample size. Let \(r_g\) denote randomization probability group \(g\), define weighted average response probability category \(\) across groups $$\bar{\pi_i} = \sum_{g=1}^{G} r_g \pi_{gi}$$ null hypothesis, \(X^2\) follows chi-square distribution \((G-1)(C-1)\) degrees freedom. alternative hypothesis, \(X^2\) follows non-central chi-square distribution non-centrality parameter $$\lambda = n \sum_{g=1}^{G} \sum_{=1}^{C}   \frac{r_g (\pi_{gi} - \bar{\pi_i})^2}   {\bar{\pi_i}}$$ sample size chosen power reject null hypothesis least \(1-\beta\) given significance level \(\alpha\).

Defaults 1 equal randomization. rounding Whether round sample size. Defaults 1 sample size rounding. kMax maximum number stages. informationRates information rates. Fixed prior trial. Defaults (1:kMax) / kMax left unspecified. efficacyStopping Indicators whether efficacy stopping allowed stage. Defaults TRUE left unspecified. futilityStopping Indicators whether futility stopping allowed stage. Defaults TRUE left unspecified. criticalValues Upper boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping efficacy. alpha significance level. Defaults 0.025. typeAlphaSpending type alpha spending. One following: "" O'Brien-Fleming boundaries, "P" Pocock boundaries, "WT" Wang & Tsiatis boundaries, "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early efficacy stopping. Defaults "sfOF". parameterAlphaSpending parameter value alpha spending. Corresponds \(\Delta\) "WT", \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". userAlphaSpending user defined alpha spending. Cumulative alpha spent stage. futilityBounds Lower boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping futility stages 1, ..., kMax-1. Defaults rep(-8, kMax-1) left unspecified. futility bounds non-binding calculation critical values. futilityCP futility bounds conditional power scale. futilitypLarger futility bounds pLarger scale. typeBetaSpending type beta spending. One following: "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early futility stopping. Defaults "none". parameterBetaSpending parameter value beta spending. Corresponds \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". userBetaSpending user defined beta spending. Cumulative beta spent stage. spendingTime vector length kMax error spending time analysis. Defaults missing, case, informationRates.

attainedAlpha: attained significance level, different overall significance level presence futility stopping.. kMax: number stages. theta: parameter value. information: maximum information. expectedInformationH1: expected information H1. expectedInformationH0: expected information H0. drift: drift parameter, equal theta*sqrt(information). inflationFactor: inflation factor (relative fixed design). numberOfSubjects: maximum number subjects. expectedNumberOfSubjectsH1: expected number subjects H1. expectedNumberOfSubjectsH0: expected number subjects H0. pLarger: probability randomly chosen sample treatment group larger randomly chosen sample control group alternative hypothesis. byStageResults: data frame containing following variables: informationRates: information rates. efficacyBounds: efficacy boundaries Z-scale. futilityBounds: futility boundaries Z-scale. rejectPerStage: probability efficacy stopping. futilityPerStage: probability futility stopping. cumulativeRejection: cumulative probability efficacy stopping. cumulativeFutility: cumulative probability futility stopping. cumulativeAlphaSpent: cumulative alpha spent. efficacyP: efficacy boundaries p-value scale. futilityP: futility boundaries p-value scale. information: cumulative information. efficacyStopping: Whether allow efficacy stopping. futilityStopping: Whether allow futility stopping. rejectPerStageH0: probability efficacy stopping H0. futilityPerStageH0: probability futility stopping H0. cumulativeRejectionH0: cumulative probability efficacy stopping H0. cumulativeFutilityH0: cumulative probability futility stopping H0. efficacyPLarger: efficacy boundaries proportion pairs samples two treatment groups sample treatment group greater control group. futilityPLarger: futility boundaries proportion pairs samples two treatment groups sample treatment group greater control group. numberOfSubjects: number subjects. settings: list containing following input parameters: typeAlphaSpending: type alpha spending. parameterAlphaSpending: parameter value alpha spending. userAlphaSpending: user defined alpha spending. typeBetaSpending: type beta spending. parameterBetaSpending: parameter value beta spending. userBetaSpending: user defined beta spending. spendingTime: error spending time analysis. allocationRatioPlanned: Allocation ratio active treatment versus control. rounding: Whether round sample size.

title: Group Sequential Design for Two-Sample Wilcoxon Test — getDesignWilcoxon
text: Mann-Whitney U test non-parametric test difference location two independent groups. also known Wilcoxon rank-sum test. test based ranks data rather actual values, making robust outliers non-normal distributions. test statistic number times randomly chosen sample treatment group larger randomly chosen sample control group, .e., $$W_{XY} = \sum_{=1}^{n_1}\sum_{j=1}^{n_2} (X_i > Y_j)$$ \(X_i\) \(Y_j\) samples treatment control groups, respectively. test often used data meet assumptions t-test, non-normality unequal variances. test also applicable ordinal data. test one-sided, meaning tests whether treatment group larger control group. Asymptotically, $$\frac{W_{XY} - n_1 n_2/2}{\sqrt{n_1 n_2 (n+1)/12}} \sim N(0,1) \quad \text{} H_0$$ \(n_1\) \(n_2\) sample sizes treatment control groups, respectively, \(n=n_1+n_2\). Let \(\theta = P(X > Y)\), \(\hat{\theta} = \frac{1}{nm}W_{XY}\). follows $$\sqrt{n}(\hat{\theta} - 1/2) \sim N\left(0, \frac{1}{12 r(1-r)}\right) \quad \text{} H_0$$ \(r = n_1/(n_1+n_2)\) randomization probability active treatment group. formulation allows group sequential testing futility stopping efficacy stopping.

title: Group Sequential Design for Two-Sample Wilcoxon Test — getDesignWilcoxon
text: Kaifeng Lu, kaifenglu@gmail.com

code: # Example 1: fixed design (design1 <- getDesignWilcoxon(   beta = 0.1, n = NA,   pLarger = pnorm((8 - 2)/sqrt(2*25^2)), alpha = 0.025)) #>                                                                               #> Fixed design for two-sample Wilcoxon test                                     #> Probability of observations in treatment larger than those in control: 0.567  #> Overall power: 0.9002, overall alpha (1-sided): 0.025                         #> Drift parameter: 3.243, inflation factor: 1                                   #> Information: 2316                                                             #> Number of subjects: 772                                                       #> Allocation ratio: 1                                                           #>                                                                               #>                                    #> Efficacy boundary (Z)       1.960  #> Efficacy boundary (pLarger) 0.5407 #> Efficacy boundary (p)       0.0250  # Example 2: group sequential design (design2 <- getDesignWilcoxon(   beta = 0.1, n = NA,   pLarger = pnorm((8 - 2)/sqrt(2*25^2)), alpha = 0.025,   kMax = 3, typeAlphaSpending = "sfOF")) #>                                                                                    #> Group-sequential design with 3 stages for two-sample Wilcoxon test                 #> Probability of observations in treatment larger than those in control: 0.567       #> Overall power: 0.9001, overall alpha (1-sided): 0.025                              #> Drift parameter: 3.261, inflation factor: 1.012                                    #> Maximum information: 2343, expected under H1: 1878.94, expected under H0: 2338.19  #> Maximum # subjects: 781, expected under H1: 626.3, expected under H0: 779.4        #> Allocation ratio: 1                                                                #> Alpha spending: Lan-DeMets O'Brien-Fleming, beta spending: None                    #>                                                                                    #>                             Stage 1 Stage 2 Stage 3 #> Information rate            0.333   0.667   1.000   #> Efficacy boundary (Z)       3.713   2.510   1.993   #> Cumulative rejection        0.0335  0.5613  0.9001  #> Cumulative alpha spent      0.0001  0.0061  0.0250  #> Number of subjects          260.0   521.0   781.0   #> Efficacy boundary (pLarger) 0.6329  0.5635  0.5412  #> Efficacy boundary (p)       0.0001  0.0060  0.0231  #> Information                 780.00  1563.00 2343.00

Provide either IMax beta; missing. theta vector length \(M\) representing true treatment effects active arm versus common control. global null \(\theta_i = 0\) \(\), alternatives one-sided: \(\theta_i > 0\) least one \(= 1, \ldots, M\). M Number active treatment arms. r Randomization ratio active arm common control. corr_known Logical. TRUE, correlation Wald statistics derived randomization ratio \(r\) \(r / (r + 1)\). FALSE, conservative correlation 0 used. kMax Number sequential looks. informationRates numeric vector information rates fixed trial. unspecified, defaults \((1:kMax) / kMax\). efficacyStopping Indicators whether efficacy stopping allowed stage. Defaults TRUE left unspecified. futilityStopping Indicators whether futility stopping allowed stage. One following: "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early futility stopping. title: Power and Sample Size for Multi-Arm Multi-Stage Design — getDesign_mams
text: S3 object class mams following components: overallResults: data frame containing: overallReject: Overall probability rejecting global null hypothesis. alpha: Overall significance level. attainedAlpha: attained significance level, different overall significance level presence futility stopping. M: Number active arms. r: Randomization ratio per active arm versus control. corr_known: Whether correlation among Wald statistics assumed known. kMax: Number stages. information: Maximum information active arm versus control. expectedInformationH1: expected information H1. expectedInformationH0: expected information H0. byStageResults: data frame containing: informationRates: Information rates analysis. efficacyBounds: Efficacy boundaries max Z-scale. futilityBounds: Futility boundaries max Z-scale. rejectPerStage: Probability efficacy stopping stage. futilityPerStage: Probability futility stopping stage. cumulativeRejection: Cumulative probability efficacy stopping. cumulativeFutility: Cumulative probability futility stopping. cumulativeAlphaSpent: Cumulative alpha spent. efficacyTheta: Efficacy boundaries parameter scale. futilityTheta: Futility boundaries parameter scale. efficacyP: Efficacy boundaries p-value scale. futilityP: Futility boundaries p-value scale. information: Cumulative information active arm versus control analysis. efficacyStopping: Indicator whether efficacy stopping permitted stage. futilityStopping: Indicator whether futility stopping permitted stage. rejectPerStageH0: probability efficacy stopping H0. futilityPerStageH0: probability futility stopping H0. cumulativeRejectionH0: cumulative probability efficacy stopping H0. cumulativeFutilityH0: cumulative probability futility stopping H0. settings: list input settings: typeAlphaSpending: type alpha spending. parameterAlphaSpending: parameter value chosen alpha spending function. userAlphaSpending: user-specified alpha spending values. typeBetaSpending: type beta spending. parameterBetaSpending: parameter value chosen beta spending function. userBetaSpending: user-specified beta spending values. spendingTime: error-spending time analysis. byLevelBounds: data frame containing efficacy boundaries level testing (.e., number active arms remaining) stage. Columns include: level: Number active arms remaining (1 \(M\)). stage: Stage index (1 kMax). efficacyBounds: Efficacy boundaries max Z-scale given level stage.

code: getDesign_seamless(   beta = NA_real_,   IMax = NA_real_,   theta = NA_real_,   M = NA_integer_,   r = 1,   corr_known = TRUE,   K = 1L,   informationRates = NA_real_,   efficacyStopping = NA_integer_,   futilityStopping = NA_integer_,   criticalValues = NULL,   alpha = 0.025,   typeAlphaSpending = "sfOF",   parameterAlphaSpending = NA_real_,   userAlphaSpending = NA_real_,   futilityBounds = NULL,   futilityCP = NULL,   futilityTheta = NULL,   typeBetaSpending = "none",   parameterBetaSpending = NA_real_,   userBetaSpending = NA_real_,   spendingTime = NA_real_ )

title: Power and Sample Size for Phase 2/3 Seamless Design — getDesign_seamless
text: beta Type II error rate. Provide either beta IMax; missing. IMax Maximum information active arm versus common control. Provide either IMax beta; missing. theta vector length \(M\) representing true treatment effects active arm versus common control. global null \(\theta_i = 0\) \(\), alternatives one-sided: \(\theta_i > 0\) least one \(= 1, \ldots, M\). M Number active treatment arms Phase 2. r Randomization ratio active arm common control Phase 2. corr_known Logical. TRUE, correlation Wald statistics Phase 2 derived randomization ratio \(r\) \(r / (r + 1)\). FALSE, conservative correlation 0 used. K Number sequential looks Phase 3. informationRates numeric vector information rates fixed trial. unspecified, defaults \((1:(K+1)) / (K+1)\). efficacyStopping Indicators whether efficacy stopping allowed stage. Defaults TRUE left unspecified. futilityStopping Indicators whether futility stopping allowed stage. Defaults TRUE left unspecified. criticalValues upper boundaries max-Z statistic scale Phase 2 Z statistics selected arm Phase 3. missing, boundaries computed based specified alpha spending function. alpha significance level. Defaults 0.025. typeAlphaSpending type alpha spending. One following: "" O'Brien-Fleming boundaries, "P" Pocock boundaries, "WT" Wang & Tsiatis boundaries, "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early efficacy stopping. Defaults "sfOF". parameterAlphaSpending parameter value alpha spending. Corresponds \(\Delta\) "WT", \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". userAlphaSpending user defined alpha spending. Cumulative alpha spent stage. futilityBounds numeric vector length \(K\) specifying futility boundaries max-Z scale end Phase 2 Z scale \(K - 1\) analyses Phase 3. final analysis uses efficacy boundary futility boundary. futilityCP numeric vector length \(K\) specifying futility boundaries conditional power scale. futilityTheta numeric vector length \(K\) specifying futility boundaries parameter scale. typeBetaSpending type beta spending. One following: "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early futility stopping. Defaults "none". parameterBetaSpending parameter value beta spending. Corresponds \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". userBetaSpending user defined beta spending. Cumulative beta spent stage. spendingTime numeric vector length \(K+1\) specifying error spending time analysis. Values must strictly increasing end 1. omitted, defaults informationRates.

title: Power and Sample Size for Phase 2/3 Seamless Design — getDesign_seamless
text: S3 object class seamless following components: overallResults: data frame containing: overallReject: Overall probability rejecting null hypothesis. alpha: Overall significance level. attainedAlpha: attained significance level, may differ alpha presence futility stopping. M: Number active arms Phase 2. r: Randomization ratio per active arm versus control Phase 2. corr_known: Whether phase-2 correlation assumed known. K: Number looks Phase 3. information: Maximum information active arm versus control. expectedInformationH1: Expected information alternative. expectedInformationH0: Expected information null. informationOverall: Maximum information overall study. expectedInformationH1: Expected information alternative overall study. expectedInformationH0: Expected information null overall study. byStageResults: data frame containing: informationRates: Information rates analysis. efficacyBounds: Efficacy boundaries Z scale. futilityBounds: Futility boundaries Z scale. rejectPerStage: Probability efficacy stopping stage. futilityPerStage: Probability futility stopping stage. cumulativeRejection: Cumulative probability efficacy stopping. cumulativeFutility: Cumulative probability futility stopping. cumulativeAlphaSpent: Cumulative alpha spent. efficacyTheta: Efficacy boundaries parameter scale. futilityTheta: Futility boundaries parameter scale. efficacyP: Efficacy boundaries p-value scale. futilityP: Futility boundaries p-value scale. information: Cumulative information analysis. informationOverall: Cumulative information overall study analysis. efficacyStopping: Indicator whether efficacy stopping permitted. futilityStopping: Indicator whether futility stopping permitted. rejectPerStageH0: Probability efficacy stopping global null. futilityPerStageH0: Probability futility stopping global null. cumulativeRejectionH0: Cumulative probability efficacy stopping global null. cumulativeFutilityH0: Cumulative probability futility stopping global null. byArmResults: data frame containing: theta: Parameter values active arms. selectAsBest: Probability arm selected best end Phase 2. powerByArm: Probability rejecting null arm trial end. condPowerByArm: Conditional power arm given selected best end Phase 2. settings: list input settings: typeAlphaSpending: Type alpha spending function. parameterAlphaSpending: Parameter value chosen alpha spending function. userAlphaSpending: User-specified alpha spending values. typeBetaSpending: Type beta spending function. parameterBetaSpending: Parameter value chosen beta spending function. userBetaSpending: User-specified beta spending values. spendingTime: Error-spending times analysis.

title: Power and Sample Size for Phase 2/3 Seamless Design — getDesign_seamless
text: corr_known FALSE, critical boundaries computed assuming independence among Phase-2 Wald statistics (conservative assumption). Power calculations, however, use correlation implied randomization ratio \(r\). Futility boundaries may supplied directly Z scale, derived conditional power, derived parameter values, computed beta spending function.

title: Range of Accrual Duration for Target Number of Events — getDurationFromNevents
code: getDurationFromNevents(   nevents = NA_real_,   allocationRatioPlanned = 1,   accrualTime = 0L,   accrualIntensity = NA_real_,   piecewiseSurvivalTime = 0L,   stratumFraction = 1L,   lambda1 = NA_real_,   lambda2 = NA_real_,   gamma1 = 0L,   gamma2 = 0L,   followupTime = NA_real_,   fixedFollowup = FALSE,   npoints = 23L )

title: Range of Accrual Duration for Target Number of Events — getDurationFromNevents
text: nevents target number events. allocationRatioPlanned Allocation ratio active treatment versus control. Defaults 1 equal randomization. accrualTime vector specifies starting time piecewise Poisson enrollment time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 3) breaks time axis 2 accrual intervals: \([0, 3)\) \([3, \infty)\). accrualIntensity vector accrual intensities. One accrual time interval. piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 6) breaks time axis 2 event intervals: \([0, 6)\) \([6, \infty)\). Defaults 0 exponential distribution. stratumFraction vector stratum fractions sum 1. Defaults 1 stratification. lambda1 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. lambda2 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum control group. gamma1 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. gamma2 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum control group. followupTime Follow-time last enrolled subjects. Must provided fixed follow-design. fixedFollowup Whether fixed follow-design used. Defaults FALSE variable follow-. npoints number accrual duration time points. Defaults 23.

title: Required Number of Events Given Hazard Ratio — getNeventsFromHazardRatio
text: beta Type II error. Defaults 0.2. kMax maximum number stages. informationRates information rates terms number events. Fixed prior trial. Defaults (1:kMax) / kMax left unspecified. efficacyStopping Indicators whether efficacy stopping allowed stage. Defaults TRUE left unspecified. futilityStopping Indicators whether futility stopping allowed stage. Defaults TRUE left unspecified. criticalValues Upper boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping efficacy. alpha significance level. Defaults 0.025. typeAlphaSpending type alpha spending. One following: "" O'Brien-Fleming boundaries, "P" Pocock boundaries, "WT" Wang & Tsiatis boundaries, "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early efficacy stopping. Defaults "sfOF". parameterAlphaSpending parameter value alpha spending. Corresponds \(\Delta\) "WT", \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". userAlphaSpending user defined alpha spending. Cumulative alpha spent stage. futilityBounds Lower boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping futility stages 1, ..., kMax-1. Defaults rep(-8, kMax-1) left unspecified. futility bounds non-binding calculation critical values. futilityCP vector length kMax - 1 futility bounds conditional power scale. futilityHR vector length kMax - 1 futility bounds hazard ratio scale. typeBetaSpending type beta spending. One following: "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early futility stopping. Defaults "none". parameterBetaSpending parameter value beta spending. Corresponds \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". userBetaSpending user defined beta spending. Cumulative beta spent stage. spendingTime vector length kMax error spending time analysis. Defaults missing, case, informationRates. hazardRatioH0 Hazard ratio null hypothesis active treatment versus control. Defaults 1 superiority test. hazardRatio Hazard ratio alternative hypothesis active treatment versus control. allocationRatioPlanned Allocation ratio active treatment versus control. Defaults 1 equal randomization. rounding Whether round number events. Defaults 1 rounding.

title: Repeated Confidence Interval for Group Sequential Design — getRCI
text: data frame following components: pvalue: Repeated p-value rejecting null hypothesis. thetahat: Point estimate parameter. cilevel: Confidence interval level. lower: Lower bound repeated confidence interval. upper: Upper bound repeated confidence interval. typeAlphaSpending "", "P", "WT", "none", informationRates, efficacyStopping, spendingTime must full length kMax, informationRates spendingTime must end 1.

title: Repeated Confidence Interval for Group Sequential Design — getRCI
text: Christopher Jennison Bruce W. Turnbull. Interim analyses: repeated confidence interval approach (discussion). J R Stat Soc Series B. 1989;51:305-361.

title: Repeated Confidence Interval for Group Sequential Design — getRCI
text: Kaifeng Lu, kaifenglu@gmail.com

title: Repeated Confidence Interval for Group Sequential Design — getRCI
code: # group sequential design with 90% power to detect delta = 6 delta <- 6 sigma <- 17 n <- 282 (des1 <- getDesign(IMax = n/(4*sigma^2), theta = delta, kMax = 3,                    alpha = 0.05, typeAlphaSpending = "sfHSD",                    parameterAlphaSpending = -4)) #>                                                                           #> Group-sequential design with 3 stages                                     #> theta: 6, maximum information: 0.24                                       #> Overall power: 0.9029, overall alpha (1-sided): 0.05                      #> Drift parameter: 2.963, inflation factor: 1.014                           #> Expected information under H1: 0.19, expected information under H0: 0.24  #> Alpha spending: HSD(gamma = -4), beta spending: None                      #>                                                                           #>                           Stage 1 Stage 2 Stage 3 #> Information rate          0.333   0.667   1.000   #> Efficacy boundary (Z)     2.794   2.289   1.680   #> Cumulative rejection      0.1395  0.5588  0.9029  #> Cumulative alpha spent    0.0026  0.0125  0.0500  #> Efficacy boundary (theta) 9.797   5.676   3.401   #> Efficacy boundary (p)     0.0026  0.0110  0.0465  #> Information               0.08    0.16    0.24     # results at the second look L <- 2 n1 <- n*2/3 delta1 <- 7 sigma1 <- 20 zL <- delta1/sqrt(4/n1*sigma1^2)  # repeated confidence interval getRCI(L = L, zL = zL, IMax = n/(4*sigma1^2),        informationRates = c(1/3, 2/3), alpha = 0.05,        typeAlphaSpending = "sfHSD", parameterAlphaSpending = -4) #>       pvalue thetahat cilevel    lower    upper #> 1 0.03741768        7     0.9 0.322283 13.67772

code: hazard_pd(   piecewiseSurvivalTime = 0L,   hazard_pfs = NA_real_,   hazard_os = NA_real_,   rho_pd_os = 0.5 )

title: Hazard Function for Progressive Disease (PD) Given Correlation Between PD and OS — hazard_pd
text: piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 6) breaks time axis 2 event intervals: \([0, 6)\) \([6, \infty)\). Defaults 0 exponential distribution. hazard_pfs scalar numeric vector specifying hazard(s) PFS based piecewise exponential distribution. hazard_os scalar numeric vector specifying hazard(s) overall survival (OS) based piecewise exponential distribution. rho_pd_os numeric value specifying correlation PD OS times.

title: Hazard Function for Progressive Disease (PD) Given Correlation Between PD and OS — hazard_pd
text: list following components: piecewiseSurvivalTime: vector specifies starting time points intervals piecewise exponential distribution PD. hazard_pd: numeric vector representing calculated hazard rates piecewise exponential distribution PD. hazard_os: numeric vector representing hazard rates piecewise exponential distribution OS time points PD. rho_pd_os: correlation PD OS times (input).

title: Hazard Function for Progressive Disease (PD) Given Correlation Between PD and OS — hazard_pd
text: function determines hazard vector \(\lambda_{\text{pd}}\) piecewise exponential distribution PD, implied survival function PFS time, \(T_{\text{pfs}} = \min(T_{\text{pd}}, T_{\text{os}})\), closely matches specified piecewise exponential distribution PFS hazard vector \(\lambda_{\text{pfs}}\). achieve , simulate \((Z_{\text{pd}}, Z_{\text{os}})\) standard bivariate normal distribution correlation \(\rho\). , \(U_{\text{pd}} = \Phi(Z_{\text{pd}})\) \(U_{\text{os}} = \Phi(Z_{\text{os}})\) generated, \(\Phi\) denotes standard normal CDF. times PD OS obtained via inverse transform method using quantile functions piecewise exponential distribution: $$T_{\text{pd}} = \text{qpwexp}(U_{\text{pd}},u,\lambda_{\text{pd}})$$ $$T_{\text{os}} = \text{qpwexp}(U_{\text{os}},u,\lambda_{\text{os}})$$ u = piecewiseSurvivalTime. function solves \(\lambda_{\text{pd}}\) survival function \(T_{\text{pfs}}\) closely matches piecewise exponential distribution hazard \(\lambda_{\text{pfs}}\): $$P(\min(T_{\text{pd}}, T_{\text{os}}) > t) = S_{\text{pfs}}(t)$$ Since $$Z_{\text{pd}} =   \Phi^{-1}(\text{ppwexp}(T_\text{pd}, u, \lambda_{\text{pd}}))$$ $$Z_{\text{os}} =   \Phi^{-1}(\text{ppwexp}(T_\text{os}, u, \lambda_{\text{os}}))$$ $$P(\min(T_{\text{pd}}, T_{\text{os}}) > t) = P(Z_{\text{pd}} >     \Phi^{-1}(\text{ppwexp}(t,u,\lambda_{\text{pd}})),   Z_{\text{os}} >     \Phi^{-1}(\text{ppwexp}(t,u,\lambda_{\text{os}})))$$ $$S_{\text{pfs}}(t) = 1 - \text{ppwexp}(t,u,\lambda_{\text{pfs}})$$ Matching performed sequentially internal cut points \(u_2, ..., u_J\) point \(u_J + \log(2)/\lambda_{\text{pfs},J}\) final interval, well percentile points 10%, 20%, ..., 90%, 95% solve \(\lambda_{\text{pd},1}, \ldots, \lambda_{\text{pd},K}\), \(K\) total number unique cut points.

title: Hazard Function for Progressive Disease (PD) Given Correlation Between PD and OS — hazard_pd
text: Kaifeng Lu (kaifenglu@gmail.com)

title: Hazard Function for Sub Population — hazard_sub
text: piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 6) breaks time axis 2 event intervals: \([0, 6)\) \([6, \infty)\). Defaults 0 exponential distribution. hazard_itt scalar numeric vector specifying hazard(s) ITT population based piecewise exponential distribution. hazard_pos scalar numeric vector specifying hazard(s) biomarker positive sub population based piecewise exponential distribution. p_pos numeric value specifying prevalence biomarker positive sub population.

title: Hazard Function for Sub Population — hazard_sub
text: list following components: piecewiseSurvivalTime: vector specifies starting time points intervals piecewise exponential distribution biomarker negative sub population. hazard_pos: numeric vector representing hazard rates piecewise exponential distribution biomarker positive sub population time points biomarker negative sub population. hazard_neg: numeric vector representing estimated hazard rates piecewise exponential distribution biomarker negative sub population. p_pos: prevalence biomarker positive sub population (input).

title: Hazard Function for Sub Population — hazard_sub
text: function determines hazard vector \(\lambda_{\text{neg}}\) piecewise exponential distribution biomarker negative sub population, implied survival function ITT population closely matches specified piecewise exponential distribution hazard vector \(\lambda_{\text{itt}}\). Let \(p_{\text{pos}}\) prevalence biomarker positive sub population, survival function ITT population given $$S_{\text{itt}}(t) = p_{\text{pos}} S_{\text{pos}}(t) + (1 - p_{\text{pos}}) S_{\text{neg}}(t)$$ \(S_{\text{pos}}(t)\) \(S_{\text{neg}}(t)\) survival functions biomarker positive biomarker negative sub populations, respectively. Matching performed sequentially internal cutpoints \(u_2, ..., u_J\) point \(u_J + \log(2)/\lambda_{\text{itt},J}\) final interval, well percentile points 10%, 20%, ..., 90%, 95%, solve \(\lambda_{\text{neg},1}, \ldots, \lambda_{\text{neg},K}\), \(K\) total number unique cut points.

text: Obtains Hedges' g estimate confidence interval effect size.

title: Hedges' g Effect Size — hedgesg
code: hedgesg(tstat, m, ntilde, cilevel = 0.95)

title: Hedges' g Effect Size — hedgesg
text: tstat value t-test statistic comparing two treatment conditions. m degrees freedom t-test. ntilde normalizing sample size convert standardized treatment difference t-test statistic, .e., tstat = sqrt(ntilde)*meanDiff/stDev. cilevel confidence interval level. Defaults 0.95.

title: Hedges' g Effect Size — hedgesg
text: data frame following variables: tstat: value t test statistic. m: degrees freedom t-test. ntilde: normalizing sample size convert standardized treatment difference t-test statistic. g: Hedges' g effect size estimate. varg: Variance g. lower: lower confidence limit effect size. upper: upper confidence limit effect size. cilevel: confidence interval level.

title: Hedges' g Effect Size — hedgesg
text: Hedges' \(g\) effect size measure commonly used meta-analysis quantify difference two groups. improvement Cohen's \(d\), particularly dealing small sample sizes. formula Hedges' \(g\) $$g = c(m) d$$ \(d\) Cohen's \(d\) effect size estimate, \(c(m)\) bias correction factor, $$d = (\hat{\mu}_1 - \hat{\mu}_2)/\hat{\sigma}$$ $$c(m) = 1 - \frac{3}{4m-1}.$$ Since \(c(m) < 1\), Cohen's \(d\) overestimates true effect size, \(\delta = (\mu_1 - \mu_2)/\sigma.\) Since $$t = \sqrt{\tilde{n}} d$$ $$g = \frac{c(m)}{\sqrt{\tilde{n}}} t$$ \(t\) noncentral \(t\) distribution \(m\) degrees freedom noncentrality parameter \(\sqrt{\tilde{n}} \delta\). asymptotic variance \(g\) can approximated $$Var(g) = \frac{1}{\tilde{n}} + \frac{g^2}{2m}.$$ confidence interval \(\delta\) can constructed using normal approximation. two-sample mean difference sample size \(n_1\) treatment group \(n_2\) control group, \(\tilde{n} = \frac{n_1n_2}{n_1+n_2}\) \(m=n_1+n_2-2\) pooled variance estimate.

title: Hedges' g Effect Size — hedgesg
text: Larry V. Hedges. Distribution theory Glass's estimator effect size related estimators. Journal Educational Statistics 1981; 6:107-128.

title: Hedges' g Effect Size — hedgesg
text: Kaifeng Lu, kaifenglu@gmail.com

title: Hedges' g Effect Size — hedgesg
code: n1 = 7 n2 = 8 meanDiff = 0.444 stDev = 1.201 m = n1+n2-2 ntilde = n1*n2/(n1+n2) tstat = sqrt(ntilde)*meanDiff/stDev  hedgesg(tstat, m, ntilde) #>       tstat  m   ntilde         g      varg      lower    upper cilevel #> 1 0.7143127 13 3.733333 0.3479453 0.2725135 -0.6752113 1.371102    0.95

code: kmpower(   kMax = 1L,   informationRates = NA_real_,   efficacyStopping = NA_integer_,   futilityStopping = NA_integer_,   criticalValues = NULL,   alpha = 0.025,   typeAlphaSpending = "sfOF",   parameterAlphaSpending = NA_real_,   userAlphaSpending = NA_real_,   futilityBounds = NULL,   futilityCP = NULL,   futilitySurvDiff = NULL,   typeBetaSpending = "none",   parameterBetaSpending = NA_real_,   milestone = NA_real_,   survDiffH0 = 0,   allocationRatioPlanned = 1,   accrualTime = 0L,   accrualIntensity = NA_real_,   piecewiseSurvivalTime = 0L,   stratumFraction = 1L,   lambda1 = NA_real_,   lambda2 = NA_real_,   gamma1 = 0L,   gamma2 = 0L,   accrualDuration = NA_real_,   followupTime = NA_real_,   fixedFollowup = FALSE,   spendingTime = NA_real_,   studyDuration = NA_real_ )

title: Power for Difference in Milestone Survival Probabilities — kmpower
text: kMax maximum number stages. informationRates information rates. Defaults (1:kMax) / kMax left unspecified. efficacyStopping Indicators whether efficacy stopping allowed stage. Defaults TRUE left unspecified. futilityStopping Indicators whether futility stopping allowed stage. Defaults TRUE left unspecified. criticalValues Upper boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping efficacy. alpha significance level. Defaults 0.025. typeAlphaSpending type alpha spending. One following: "" O'Brien-Fleming boundaries, "P" Pocock boundaries, "WT" Wang & Tsiatis boundaries, "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early efficacy stopping. Defaults "sfOF". parameterAlphaSpending parameter value alpha spending. Corresponds \(\Delta\) "WT", \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". userAlphaSpending user defined alpha spending. Cumulative alpha spent stage. futilityBounds Lower boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping futility stages 1, ..., kMax-1. Defaults rep(-8, kMax-1) left unspecified. futility bounds non-binding calculation critical values. futilityCP vector length kMax - 1 futility bounds conditional power scale. futilitySurvDiff vector length kMax - 1 futility bounds milestone survival difference scale. typeBetaSpending type beta spending. One following: "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "none" early futility stopping. Defaults "none". parameterBetaSpending parameter value beta spending. Corresponds \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". milestone milestone time calculate survival probability. survDiffH0 difference milestone survival probabilities null hypothesis. Defaults 0 superiority test. allocationRatioPlanned Allocation ratio active treatment versus control. Defaults 1 equal randomization. accrualTime vector specifies starting time piecewise Poisson enrollment time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 3) breaks time axis 2 accrual intervals: \([0, 3)\) \([3, \infty)\). accrualIntensity vector accrual intensities. One accrual time interval. piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 6) breaks time axis 2 event intervals: \([0, 6)\) \([6, \infty)\). Defaults 0 exponential distribution. stratumFraction vector stratum fractions sum 1. Defaults 1 stratification. lambda1 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. lambda2 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum control group. gamma1 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. gamma2 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum control group. accrualDuration Duration enrollment period. followupTime Follow-time last enrolled subject. fixedFollowup Whether fixed follow-design used. Defaults FALSE variable follow-. spendingTime vector length kMax error spending time analysis. Defaults missing, case, informationRates. studyDuration Study duration fixed follow-design. Defaults missing, replaced sum accrualDuration followupTime. provided, value allowed less sum accrualDuration followupTime.

events: 356.4, expected # events: 325.3                           #> Maximum # subjects: 468, expected # subjects: 468                           #> Maximum information: 527.17, expected information: 467.35                   #> Total study duration: 40, expected study duration: 34.9                     #> Accrual duration: 22, follow-up duration: 18, fixed follow-up: FALSE        #> Allocation ratio: 1                                                         #> Alpha spending: Lan-DeMets O'Brien-Fleming, beta spending: None             #>                                                                             #>                               Stage 1 Stage 2 #> Information rate              0.800   1.000   #> Efficacy boundary (Z)         2.250   2.025   #> Cumulative rejection          0.5674  0.7634  #> Cumulative alpha spent        0.0122  0.0250  #> Number of events              301.5   356.4   #> Number of dropouts            19.8    24.2    #> Number of subjects            468.0   468.0   #> Number of milestone subjects  71.1    140.9   #> Analysis time                 31.1    40.0    #> Efficacy boundary (surv diff) 0.110   0.088   #> Efficacy boundary (p)         0.0122  0.0214  #> Information                   421.74  527.17

text: beta Type II error. Defaults 0.2. kMax maximum number stages. informationRates information rates. Defaults (1:kMax) / kMax left unspecified. efficacyStopping Indicators whether efficacy stopping allowed stage. Defaults TRUE left unspecified. futilityStopping Indicators whether futility stopping allowed stage. Defaults TRUE left unspecified. criticalValues Upper boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping efficacy. alpha significance level. Defaults 0.025. typeAlphaSpending type alpha spending. One following: "" O'Brien-Fleming boundaries, "P" Pocock boundaries, "WT" Wang & Tsiatis boundaries, "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early efficacy stopping. Defaults "sfOF". parameterAlphaSpending parameter value alpha spending. Corresponds \(\Delta\) "WT", \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". userAlphaSpending user defined alpha spending. Cumulative alpha spent stage. futilityBounds Lower boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping futility stages 1, ..., kMax-1. Defaults rep(-8, kMax-1) left unspecified. futility bounds non-binding calculation critical values. futilityCP vector length kMax - 1 futility bounds conditional power scale. futilitySurvDiff vector length kMax - 1 futility bounds milestone survival difference scale. typeBetaSpending type beta spending. One following: "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early futility stopping. Defaults "none". parameterBetaSpending parameter value beta spending. Corresponds \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". userBetaSpending user defined beta spending. Cumulative beta spent stage. milestone milestone time calculate survival probability. survDiffH0 difference milestone survival probabilities null hypothesis. Defaults 0 superiority test. allocationRatioPlanned Allocation ratio active treatment versus control. Defaults 1 equal randomization. accrualTime vector specifies starting time piecewise Poisson enrollment time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 3) breaks time axis 2 accrual intervals: \([0, 3)\) \([3, \infty)\). accrualIntensity vector accrual intensities. One accrual time interval. piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Defaults 1 stratification. lambda1 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. lambda2 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum control group. gamma1 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. gamma2 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum control group. accrualDuration Duration enrollment period. followupTime Follow-time last enrolled subject. fixedFollowup Whether fixed follow-design used. title: Sample Size for One-Sample Milestone Survival Probability — kmsamplesize1s
code: kmsamplesize1s(   beta = 0.2,   kMax = 1L,   informationRates = NA_real_,   efficacyStopping = NA_integer_,   futilityStopping = NA_integer_,   criticalValues = NULL,   alpha = 0.025,   typeAlphaSpending = "sfOF",   parameterAlphaSpending = NA_real_,   userAlphaSpending = NA_real_,   futilityBounds = NULL,   futilityCP = NULL,   futilitySurv = NULL,   typeBetaSpending = "none",   parameterBetaSpending = NA_real_,   userBetaSpending = NA_real_,   milestone = NA_real_,   survH0 = NA_real_,   accrualTime = 0L,   accrualIntensity = NA_real_,   piecewiseSurvivalTime = 0L,   stratumFraction = 1L,   lambda = NA_real_,   gamma = 0L,   accrualDuration = NA_real_,   followupTime = NA_real_,   fixedFollowup = FALSE,   spendingTime = NA_real_,   rounding = TRUE )

title: Sample Size for One-Sample Milestone Survival Probability — kmsamplesize1s
text: beta Type II error. Defaults 0.2. kMax maximum number stages. informationRates information rates. Defaults (1:kMax) / kMax left unspecified. efficacyStopping Indicators whether efficacy stopping allowed stage. Defaults TRUE left unspecified. futilityStopping Indicators whether futility stopping allowed stage. Defaults TRUE left unspecified. criticalValues Upper boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping efficacy. alpha significance level. Defaults 0.025. typeAlphaSpending type alpha spending. One following: "" O'Brien-Fleming boundaries, "P" Pocock boundaries, "WT" Wang & Tsiatis boundaries, "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early efficacy stopping. Defaults "sfOF". parameterAlphaSpending parameter value alpha spending. Corresponds \(\Delta\) "WT", \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". userAlphaSpending user defined alpha spending. Cumulative alpha spent stage. futilityBounds Lower boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping futility stages 1, ..., kMax-1. Defaults rep(-8, kMax-1) left unspecified. futility bounds non-binding calculation critical values. futilityCP vector length kMax - 1 futility bounds conditional power scale. futilitySurv vector length kMax - 1 futility bounds milestone survival scale. typeBetaSpending type beta spending. One following: "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early futility stopping. Defaults "none". parameterBetaSpending parameter value beta spending. Corresponds \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". userBetaSpending user defined beta spending. Cumulative beta spent stage. milestone milestone time calculate survival probability. survH0 milestone survival probability null hypothesis. accrualTime vector specifies starting time piecewise Poisson enrollment time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 3) breaks time axis 2 accrual intervals: \([0, 3)\) \([3, \infty)\). accrualIntensity vector accrual intensities. One accrual time interval. piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 6) breaks time axis 2 event intervals: \([0, 6)\) \([6, \infty)\). Defaults 0 exponential distribution. stratumFraction vector stratum fractions sum 1. Defaults 1 stratification. lambda vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum alternative hypothesis. gamma hazard rate exponential dropout vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout. Defaults 0 dropout. accrualDuration Duration enrollment period. followupTime Follow-time last enrolled subject. fixedFollowup Whether fixed follow-design used. Defaults FALSE variable follow-. spendingTime vector length kMax error spending time analysis. Defaults missing, case, informationRates. rounding Whether round sample size. Defaults 1 sample size rounding.

title: Sample Size for Equivalence in Milestone Survival Probability Difference — kmsamplesizeequiv
text: beta type II error. kMax maximum number stages. informationRates information rates. Defaults (1:kMax) / kMax left unspecified. criticalValues Upper boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping efficacy. alpha significance level two one-sided tests. Defaults 0.05. typeAlphaSpending type alpha spending. One following: "" O'Brien-Fleming boundaries, "P" Pocock boundaries, "WT" Wang & Tsiatis boundaries, "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early efficacy stopping. Defaults "sfOF". parameterAlphaSpending parameter value alpha spending. Corresponds \(\Delta\) "WT", \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". userAlphaSpending user defined alpha spending. Cumulative alpha spent stage. milestone milestone time calculate survival probability. survDiffLower lower equivalence limit milestone survival probability difference. survDiffUpper upper equivalence limit milestone survival probability difference. allocationRatioPlanned Allocation ratio active treatment versus control. Defaults 1 equal randomization. accrualTime vector specifies starting time piecewise Poisson enrollment time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 3) breaks time axis 2 accrual intervals: \([0, 3)\) \([3, \infty)\). accrualIntensity vector accrual intensities. One accrual time interval. piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 6) breaks time axis 2 event intervals: \([0, 6)\) \([6, \infty)\). Defaults 0 exponential distribution. stratumFraction vector stratum fractions sum 1. Defaults 1 stratification. lambda1 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. lambda2 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum control group. gamma1 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. gamma2 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum control group. accrualDuration Duration enrollment period. followupTime Follow-time last enrolled subject. fixedFollowup Whether fixed follow-design used. Defaults FALSE variable follow-. spendingTime vector length kMax error spending time analysis. Defaults missing, case, informationRates. rounding Whether round sample size. Defaults 1 sample size rounding.

title: Stratified Difference in Milestone Survival Probabilities — kmstat
text: time vector calendar times data cut. milestone milestone time calculate survival probability. allocationRatioPlanned Allocation ratio active treatment versus control. Defaults 1 equal randomization. accrualTime vector specifies starting time piecewise Poisson enrollment time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 3) breaks time axis 2 accrual intervals: \([0, 3)\) \([3, \infty)\). accrualIntensity vector accrual intensities. One accrual time interval. piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 6) breaks time axis 2 event intervals: \([0, 6)\) \([6, \infty)\). Defaults 0 exponential distribution. stratumFraction vector stratum fractions sum 1. Defaults 1 stratification. lambda1 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. lambda2 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum control group. gamma1 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. gamma2 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum control group. accrualDuration Duration enrollment period. followupTime Follow-time last enrolled subject. fixedFollowup Whether fixed follow-design used. Defaults FALSE variable follow-.

title: Stratified Difference in Milestone Survival Probabilities — kmstat
text: data frame containing following variables: time: calendar time since trial start. subjects: number enrolled subjects. nevents: total number events. nevents1: number events active treatment group. nevents2: number events control group. ndropouts: total number dropouts. ndropouts1: number dropouts active treatment group. ndropouts2: number dropouts control group. milestone: milestone time relative randomization. nmilestone: total number subjects reaching milestone. nmilestone1: number subjects reaching milestone active treatment group. nmiletone2: number subjects reaching milestone control group. surv1: milestone survival probability treatment group. surv2: milestone survival probability control group. survDiff: difference milestone survival probabilities, .e., surv1 - surv2. vsurv1: variance surv1. vsurv2: variance surv2. vsurvDiff: variance survDiff. information: information survDiff, equal 1/vsurvDiff. survDiffZ: Z-statistic value, .e., survDiff/sqrt(vsurvDiff).

title: Parametric Regression Models for Failure Time Data — liferegr
text: data input data frame contains following variables: stratum: stratum. time: follow-time right censored data, left end interval interval censored data. time2: right end interval interval censored data. event: event indicator, 1=event, 0=event. covariates: values baseline covariates. weight: weight observation. offset: offset observation. id: optional subject ID group score residuals computing robust sandwich variance. stratum name(s) stratum variable(s) input data. time name time variable left end interval interval censored data input data. time2 name right end interval interval censored data input data. event name event variable input data right censored data. covariates vector names baseline covariates input data. weight name weight variable input data. offset name offset variable input data. id name id variable input data. dist assumed distribution time event. Options include "exponential", "weibull", "lognormal", "loglogistic" modeled log-scale, "normal" "logistic" modeled original scale. init vector initial values model parameters, including regression coefficients log scale parameter. default, initial values derived intercept-model. approach fails, ordinary least squares (OLS) estimates, ignoring censoring, used instead. robust Whether robust sandwich variance estimate computed. presence id variable, score residuals aggregated id computing robust sandwich variance estimate. plci Whether obtain profile likelihood confidence interval. alpha two-sided significance level. maxiter maximum number iterations. eps tolerance declare convergence.

title: Parametric Regression Models for Failure Time Data — liferegr
text: list following components: sumstat: data frame summary statistics model fit following variables: n: number observations. nevents: number events. loglik0: log-likelihood null. loglik1: maximum log-likelihood. niter: number Newton-Raphson iterations. dist: assumed distribution. p: number parameters, including intercept, regression coefficients associated covariates, log scale parameters strata. nvar: number regression coefficients associated covariates (excluding intercept). robust: Whether robust sandwich variance estimate requested. fail: Whether model fails converge. parest: data frame parameter estimates following variables: param: name covariate parameter estimate. beta: parameter estimate. sebeta: standard error parameter estimate. z: Wald test statistic parameter. expbeta: exponentiated parameter estimate. lower: lower limit confidence interval. upper: upper limit confidence interval. p: p-value chi-square test. method: method compute confidence interval p-value. sebeta_naive: naive standard error parameter estimate robust variance requested. linear_predictors: vector linear predictors. p: number parameters. nvar: number columns design matrix excluding intercept. param: parameter names. beta: parameter estimate. vbeta: covariance matrix parameter estimates. vbeta_naive: naive covariance matrix parameter estimates. terms: terms object. xlevels: record levels factors used fitting. settings: list containing input parameter values.

title: Parametric Regression Models for Failure Time Data — liferegr
text: two ways specify model, one right censored data time event variables, interval censored data time (lower) time2 (upper) variables. second form, follow convention used SAS PROC LIFEREG: lower missing, upper missing, lower equal upper, censoring event occurred time lower. lower missing, upper missing, lower < upper, event time censored within interval (lower, upper). lower missing, upper missing, upper used left censoring value. lower missing, upper missing, lower used right censoring value. lower missing, upper missing, lower > upper, lower upper missing, observation used.

text: data input data frame contains following variables: event: event indicator, 1=event, 0=event. covariates: values baseline covariates. freq: frequency observation. weight: weight observation. offset: offset observation. id: optional subject ID group score residuals computing robust sandwich variance. event name event variable input data. covariates vector names baseline covariates input data. freq name frequency variable input data. frequencies must observations within cluster indicated id. title: Logistic Regression Models for Binary Data — logisregr
text: Fitting logistic regression model using Firth's bias reduction method equivalent penalization log-likelihood Jeffreys prior. Firth's penalized log-likelihood given $$l(\beta) + \frac{1}{2} \log(\mbox{det}((\beta)))$$ components gradient \(g(\beta)\) computed $$g(\beta_j) + \frac{1}{2} \mbox{trace}\left((\beta)^{-1} \frac{\partial (\beta)}{\partial \beta_j}\right)$$ Hessian matrix modified penalty. Firth's method reduces bias maximum likelihood estimates coefficients, introduces bias toward one-half predicted probabilities. straightforward modification Firth’s logistic regression achieve unbiased average predicted probabilities involves post hoc adjustment intercept. approach, known Firth’s logistic regression intercept correction (FLIC), preserves bias-corrected effect estimates. excluding intercept penalization, ensures sacrifice accuracy effect estimates improve predictions.
