title: Log-Rank Test Power — lrpower
code: lrpower(   kMax = 1L,   informationRates = NA_real_,   efficacyStopping = NA_integer_,   futilityStopping = NA_integer_,   criticalValues = NULL,   alpha = 0.025,   typeAlphaSpending = "sfOF",   parameterAlphaSpending = NA_real_,   userAlphaSpending = NA_real_,   futilityBounds = NULL,   futilityCP = NULL,   futilityHR = NULL,   typeBetaSpending = "none",   parameterBetaSpending = NA_real_,   hazardRatioH0 = 1,   allocationRatioPlanned = 1,   accrualTime = 0L,   accrualIntensity = NA_real_,   piecewiseSurvivalTime = 0L,   stratumFraction = 1L,   lambda1 = NA_real_,   lambda2 = NA_real_,   gamma1 = 0L,   gamma2 = 0L,   accrualDuration = NA_real_,   followupTime = NA_real_,   fixedFollowup = FALSE,   rho1 = 0,   rho2 = 0,   typeOfComputation = "",   spendingTime = NA_real_,   studyDuration = NA_real_ )

title: Log-Rank Test Power — lrpower
text: kMax maximum number stages. informationRates information rates terms number events conventional log-rank test terms actual information weighted log-rank tests. Defaults (1:kMax) / kMax left unspecified. efficacyStopping Indicators whether efficacy stopping allowed stage. Defaults TRUE left unspecified. futilityStopping Indicators whether futility stopping allowed stage. Defaults TRUE left unspecified. criticalValues Upper boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping efficacy. alpha significance level. Defaults 0.025. typeAlphaSpending type alpha spending. One following: "" O'Brien-Fleming boundaries, "P" Pocock boundaries, "WT" Wang & Tsiatis boundaries, "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early efficacy stopping. Defaults "sfOF". parameterAlphaSpending parameter value alpha spending. Corresponds \(\Delta\) "WT", \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". userAlphaSpending user defined alpha spending. Cumulative alpha spent stage. futilityBounds Lower boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping futility stages 1, ..., kMax-1. Defaults rep(-8, kMax-1) left unspecified. futility bounds non-binding calculation critical values. futilityCP vector length kMax - 1 futility bounds conditional power scale. futilityHR vector length kMax - 1 futility bounds hazard ratio scale. typeBetaSpending type beta spending. One following: "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "none" early futility stopping. Defaults "none". parameterBetaSpending parameter value beta spending. Corresponds \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". hazardRatioH0 Hazard ratio null hypothesis active treatment versus control. Defaults 1 superiority test. allocationRatioPlanned Allocation ratio active treatment versus control. Defaults 1 equal randomization. accrualTime vector specifies starting time piecewise Poisson enrollment time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 3) breaks time axis 2 accrual intervals: \([0, 3)\) \([3, \infty)\). accrualIntensity vector accrual intensities. One accrual time interval. piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 6) breaks time axis 2 event intervals: \([0, 6)\) \([6, \infty)\). Defaults 0 exponential distribution. stratumFraction vector stratum fractions sum 1. Defaults 1 stratification. lambda1 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. lambda2 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum control group. gamma1 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. gamma2 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum control group. accrualDuration Duration enrollment period. followupTime Follow-time last enrolled subject. fixedFollowup Whether fixed follow-design used. Defaults FALSE variable follow-. rho1 first parameter Fleming-Harrington family weighted log-rank test. Defaults 0 conventional log-rank test. rho2 second parameter Fleming-Harrington family weighted log-rank test. Defaults 0 conventional log-rank test. typeOfComputation type computation, either "direct" direct approximation method, "schoenfeld" Schoenfeld method. Defaults empty, selects Schoenfeld method proportional hazards ordinary log-rank test direct method otherwise. spendingTime vector length kMax error spending time analysis. Defaults missing, case, informationRates. studyDuration Study duration fixed follow-design. Defaults missing, replaced sum accrualDuration followupTime. provided, value allowed less sum accrualDuration followupTime.

title: Log-Rank Test Power — lrpower
text: S3 class lrpower object 4 components: overallResults: data frame containing following variables: overallReject: overall rejection probability. alpha: overall significance level. numberOfEvents: total number events. numberOfDropouts: total number dropouts. numbeOfSubjects: total number subjects. studyDuration: total study duration. information: maximum information. expectedNumberOfEvents: expected number events. expectedNumberOfDropouts: expected number dropouts. expectedNumberOfSubjects: expected number subjects. expectedStudyDuration: expected study duration. expectedInformation: expected information. accrualDuration: accrual duration. followupTime: follow-time. fixedFollowup: Whether fixed follow-design used. rho1: first parameter Fleming-Harrington family weighted log-rank test. rho2: second parameter Fleming-Harrington family weighted log-rank test. kMax: number stages. hazardRatioH0: hazard ratio null hypothesis. typeOfComputation: type computation, either "direct" direct approximation method, "schoenfeld" Schoenfeld method. byStageResults: data frame containing following variables: informationRates: information rates. efficacyBounds: efficacy boundaries Z-scale. futilityBounds: futility boundaries Z-scale. rejectPerStage: probability efficacy stopping. futilityPerStage: probability futility stopping. cumulativeRejection: cumulative probability efficacy stopping. cumulativeFutility: cumulative probability futility stopping. cumulativeAlphaSpent: cumulative alpha spent. numberOfEvents: number events. numberOfDropouts: number dropouts. numberOfSubjects: number subjects. analysisTime: average time since trial start. efficacyHR: efficacy boundaries hazard ratio scale. futilityHR: futility boundaries hazard ratio scale. efficacyP: efficacy boundaries p-value scale. futilityP: futility boundaries p-value scale. information: cumulative information. HR: average hazard ratio. efficacyStopping: Whether allow efficacy stopping. futilityStopping: Whether allow futility stopping. settings: list containing following input parameters: typeAlphaSpending, parameterAlphaSpending, userAlphaSpending, typeBetaSpending, parameterBetaSpending, allocationRatioPlanned, accrualTime, accuralIntensity, piecewiseSurvivalTime, stratumFraction, lambda1, lambda2, gamma1, gamma2, spendingTime. byTreatmentCounts: list containing following counts treatment group: numberOfEvents1: number events stage treatment group. numberOfDropouts1: number dropouts stage treatment group. numberOfSubjects1: number subjects stage treatment group. numberOfEvents2: number events stage control group. numberOfDropouts2: number dropouts stage control group. numberOfSubjects2: number subjects stage control group. expectedNumberOfEvents1: expected number events treatment group. expectedNumberOfDropouts1: expected number dropouts treatment group. expectedNumberOfSubjects1: expected number subjects treatment group. expectedNumberOfEvents2: expected number events control group. expectedNumberOfDropouts2: expected number dropouts control group. expectedNumberOfSubjects2: expected number subjects control group.

HR: average hazard ratio. settings: list containing following input parameters: typeAlphaSpending, parameterAlphaSpending, userAlphaSpending, allocationRatioPlanned, accrualTime, accuralIntensity, piecewiseSurvivalTime, stratumFraction, lambda1, lambda2, gamma1, gamma2, typeOfComputation, spendingTime. byTreatmentCounts: list containing following counts treatment group: numberOfEvents1: number events stage treatment group. numberOfDropouts1: number dropouts stage treatment group. numberOfSubjects1: number subjects stage treatment group. numberOfEvents2: number events stage control group. numberOfDropouts2: number dropouts stage control group. numberOfSubjects2: number subjects stage control group. expectedNumberOfEvents1: expected number events treatment group. expectedNumberOfDropouts1: expected number dropouts treatment group. expectedNumberOfSubjects1: expected number subjects treatment group. expectedNumberOfEvents2: expected number events control group. expectedNumberOfDropouts2: expected number dropouts control group. expectedNumberOfSubjects2: expected number subjects control group.

title: Log-Rank Test Sample Size — lrsamplesize
text: beta Type II error. Defaults 0.2. kMax maximum number stages. informationRates information rates terms number events conventional log-rank test terms actual information weighted log-rank tests. Defaults (1:kMax) / kMax left unspecified. efficacyStopping Indicators whether efficacy stopping allowed stage. Defaults TRUE left unspecified. futilityStopping Indicators whether futility stopping allowed stage. Defaults TRUE left unspecified. criticalValues Upper boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping efficacy. alpha significance level. Defaults 0.025. typeAlphaSpending type alpha spending. One following: "" O'Brien-Fleming boundaries, "P" Pocock boundaries, "WT" Wang & Tsiatis boundaries, "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early efficacy stopping. Defaults "sfOF". parameterAlphaSpending parameter value alpha spending. Corresponds \(\Delta\) "WT", \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". userAlphaSpending user defined alpha spending. Cumulative alpha spent stage. futilityBounds Lower boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping futility stages 1, ..., kMax-1. Defaults rep(-8, kMax-1) left unspecified. futility bounds non-binding calculation critical values. futilityCP vector length kMax - 1 futility bounds conditional power scale. futilityHR vector length kMax - 1 futility bounds hazard ratio scale. typeBetaSpending type beta spending. One following: "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early futility stopping. Defaults "none". parameterBetaSpending parameter value beta spending. Corresponds \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". userBetaSpending user defined beta spending. Cumulative beta spent stage. hazardRatioH0 Hazard ratio null hypothesis active treatment versus control. Defaults 1 superiority test. allocationRatioPlanned Allocation ratio active treatment versus control. Defaults 1 equal randomization. accrualTime vector specifies starting time piecewise Poisson enrollment time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 3) breaks time axis 2 accrual intervals: \([0, 3)\) \([3, \infty)\). accrualIntensity vector accrual intensities. One accrual time interval. piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 6) breaks time axis 2 event intervals: \([0, 6)\) \([6, \infty)\). Defaults 0 exponential distribution. stratumFraction vector stratum fractions sum 1. Defaults 1 stratification. lambda1 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. lambda2 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum control group. gamma1 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. gamma2 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum control group. accrualDuration Duration enrollment period. followupTime Follow-time last enrolled subject. fixedFollowup Whether fixed follow-design used. Defaults FALSE variable follow-. rho1 first parameter Fleming-Harrington family weighted log-rank test. Defaults 0 conventional log-rank test. rho2 second parameter Fleming-Harrington family weighted log-rank test. Defaults 0 conventional log-rank test. typeOfComputation type computation, either "direct" direct approximation method, "schoenfeld" Schoenfeld method. Defaults empty, selects Schoenfeld method proportional hazards ordinary log-rank test direct method otherwise. spendingTime vector length kMax error spending time analysis. Defaults missing, case, informationRates. rounding Whether round sample size events. Defaults 1 sample size rounding.

Cumulative alpha spent stage. futilityBounds Lower boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping futility stages 1, ..., kMax-1. Defaults rep(-8, kMax-1) left unspecified. futility bounds non-binding calculation critical values. futilityCP vector length kMax - 1 futility bounds conditional power scale. futilityHR vector length kMax - 1 futility bounds hazard ratio scale. typeBetaSpending type beta spending. One following: "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early futility stopping. Defaults "none". parameterBetaSpending parameter value beta spending. Corresponds \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". userBetaSpending user defined beta spending. Cumulative beta spent stage. hazardRatioH0 Hazard ratio null hypothesis active treatment versus control. Defaults 1 superiority test. allocationRatioPlanned Allocation ratio active treatment versus control. Defaults 1 equal randomization. accrualTime vector specifies starting time piecewise Poisson enrollment time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 3) breaks time axis 2 accrual intervals: \([0, 3)\) \([3, \infty)\). accrualIntensity vector accrual intensities. One accrual time interval. piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 6) breaks time axis 2 event intervals: \([0, 6)\) \([6, \infty)\). Defaults 0 exponential distribution. stratumFraction vector stratum fractions sum 1. Defaults 1 stratification. hazardRatio Hazard ratio alternative hypothesis active treatment versus control. lambda2 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum control group. gamma1 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. gamma2 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum control group. followupTime Follow-time last enrolled subject. fixedFollowup Whether fixed follow-design used. Defaults FALSE variable follow-. spendingTime vector length kMax error spending time analysis. Defaults missing, case, informationRates. rounding Whether round sample size events. Defaults 1 sample size rounding. calibrate Whether use simulations calibrate number events calculated using Schoenfeld formula. maxNumberOfIterations number simulation iterations. Defaults 10000. maxNumberOfRawDatasetsPerStage number raw datasets per stage extract. seed seed reproduce simulation results. seed environment used left unspecified.

title: Schoenfeld Method for Log-Rank Test Sample Size Calculation — lrschoenfeld
text: list two components: analyticalResults: S3 class lrpower object asymptotic power. simulationResults: S3 class lrsim object empirical power.

title: Schoenfeld Method for Log-Rank Test Sample Size Calculation — lrschoenfeld
text: function calculates sample size study duration calibrating number events estimated using Schoenfeld formula proportional hazards assumption, particularly hazard ratio far away one /allocation groups unequal. fixed design, Schoenfeld formula required number events $$D = \frac{(\Phi^{-1}(1-\alpha) + \Phi^{-1}(1-\beta))^2} {(\theta - \theta_0)^2 r(1-r)}$$ \(D\) total number events required, \(\alpha\) type error rate, \(\beta\) type II error rate, \(r\) randomization probability active treatment group, \(\theta_0\) \(\theta\) log hazard ratios null alternative hypotheses, respectively. function first computes number events using Schoenfeld formula. calibrate set 1, function uses simulations calibrate number events, accounting scenarios Schoenfeld formula may inaccurate (e.g., allocation unequal hazard ratio extreme). Let \(D_{schoenfeld}\) number events calculated Schoenfeld formula, \(D_{calibrated}\) calibrated number events. calibrated number events calculated #' $$D_{\text{calibrated}} = \frac{\left\{\Phi^{-1}(1-\alpha) + \Phi^{-1}(1-\beta)\right\}^2} {\left\{\Phi^{-1}(1-\alpha) + \Phi^{-1}(1-\beta_{\text{schoenfeld}})\right\}^2} D_{\text{schoenfeld}}$$ \(\beta_{schoenfeld}\) empirical type II error estimated via simulation. second round simulation performed obtain empirical power using calibrated number events.

title: Schoenfeld Method for Log-Rank Test Sample Size Calculation — lrschoenfeld

title: Log-Rank Test Simulation — lrsim
text: Performs simulation two-arm group sequential trials based weighted log-rank test.

title: Log-Rank Test Simulation — lrsim
code: lrsim(   kMax = 1,   informationRates = NA,   criticalValues = NA,   futilityBounds = NA,   hazardRatioH0 = 1,   allocation1 = 1,   allocation2 = 1,   accrualTime = 0,   accrualIntensity = NA,   piecewiseSurvivalTime = 0,   stratumFraction = 1,   lambda1 = NA,   lambda2 = NA,   gamma1 = 0,   gamma2 = 0,   n = NA,   followupTime = NA,   fixedFollowup = FALSE,   rho1 = 0,   rho2 = 0,   plannedEvents = NA,   plannedTime = NA,   maxNumberOfIterations = 1000,   maxNumberOfRawDatasetsPerStage = 0,   seed = 0,   nthreads = 0 )

title: Log-Rank Test Simulation — lrsim
text: kMax maximum number stages. informationRates information rates terms number events conventional log-rank test terms actual information weighted log-rank tests. Fixed prior trial. left unspecified, defaults plannedEvents / plannedEvents[kMax] plannedEvents provided plannedTime / plannedTime[kMax] otherwise. criticalValues Upper boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping efficacy. futilityBounds Lower boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping futility stages 1, ..., kMax-1. Defaults rep(-8, kMax-1) left unspecified. futility bounds non-binding calculation critical values. hazardRatioH0 Hazard ratio null hypothesis active treatment versus control. Defaults 1 superiority test. allocation1 Number subjects active treatment group randomization block. Defaults 1 equal randomization. allocation2 Number subjects control group randomization block. Defaults 1 equal randomization. accrualTime vector specifies starting time piecewise Poisson enrollment time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 3) breaks time axis 2 accrual intervals: \([0, 3)\) \([3, \infty)\). accrualIntensity vector accrual intensities. One accrual time interval. piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 6) breaks time axis 2 event intervals: \([0, 6)\) \([6, \infty)\). Defaults 0 exponential distribution. stratumFraction vector stratum fractions sum 1. Defaults 1 stratification. lambda1 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. lambda2 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum control group. gamma1 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. gamma2 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum control group. n Sample size. followupTime Follow-time last enrolled subject. fixedFollowup Whether fixed follow-design used. Defaults FALSE variable follow-. rho1 first parameter Fleming-Harrington family weighted log-rank test. Defaults 0 conventional log-rank test. rho2 second parameter Fleming-Harrington family weighted log-rank test. Defaults 0 conventional log-rank test. plannedEvents planned cumulative total number events stage. plannedTime calendar times analyses. use calendar time plan analyses, plannedEvents missing. maxNumberOfIterations number simulation iterations. Defaults 1000. maxNumberOfRawDatasetsPerStage number raw datasets per stage extract. seed seed reproduce simulation results. nthreads number threads use simulations (0 means default RcppParallel behavior).

title: Log-Rank Test Simulation — lrsim
text: S3 class lrsim object 3 components: overview: list containing following information: rejectPerStage: efficacy stopping probability stage. futilityPerStage: futility stopping probability stage. cumulativeRejection: Cumulative efficacy stopping probability stage. cumulativeFutility: cumulative futility stopping probability stage. numberOfEvents: average number events stage. numberOfDropouts: average number dropouts stage. numberOfSubjects: average number subjects stage. analysisTime: average analysis time stage. overallReject: overall rejection probability. expectedNumberOfEvents: expected number events overall study. expectedNumberOfDropouts: expected number dropouts overall study. expectedNumberOfSubjects: expected number subjects overall study. expectedStudyDuration: expected study duration. hazardRatioH0: Hazard ratio null hypothesis active treatment versus control. useEvents: whether analyses planned based number events calendar time. numberOfIterations: number simulation iterations. n: Sample size. fixedFollowup: Whether fixed follow-design used. rho1: first parameter Fleming-Harrington family weighted log-rank test. Defaults 0 conventional log-rank test. rho2: second parameter Fleming-Harrington family weighted log-rank test. Defaults 0 conventional log-rank test. kMax: maximum number stages. sumdata: data frame summary data iteration stage: iterationNumber: iteration number. eventsNotAchieved: Whether final target number events achieved iteration. stopStage: stage trial stops. stageNumber: stage number, covering stages even trial stops interim look. analysisTime: time stage since trial start. accruals1: number subjects enrolled stage treatment group. accruals2: number subjects enrolled stage control group. totalAccruals: total number subjects enrolled stage. events1: number events stage treatment group. events2: number events stage control group. totalEvents: total number events stage. dropouts1: number dropouts stage treatment group. dropouts2: number dropouts stage control group. totalDropouts: total number dropouts stage. uscore: numerator log-rank test statistic. vscore: variance log-rank test statistic. logRankStatistic: log-rank test Z-statistic. rejectPerStage: Whether reject null hypothesis stage. futilityPerStage: Whether stop trial futility stage. rawdata (exists maxNumberOfRawDatasetsPerStage positive integer): data frame subject-level data selected replications, containing following variables: iterationNumber: iteration number. stopStage: stage trial stops. stageNumber: stage number, covering stages even trial stops interim look. analysisTime: time stage since trial start. subjectId: subject ID. arrivalTime: enrollment time subject. stratum: stratum subject. treatmentGroup: treatment group (1 2) subject. survivalTime: underlying survival time subject. dropoutTime: underlying dropout time subject. timeUnderObservation: time observation since randomization. event: Whether subject experienced event. dropoutEvent: Whether subject dropped .

what: Log-Rank Test Simulation for PFS and OS Endpoints — lrsim2e
title: Log-Rank Test Simulation for PFS and OS Endpoints — lrsim2e
text: Performs simulation two-endpoint (PFS OS) two-arm group sequential trials based weighted log-rank test. first kMaxpfs looks driven total number PFS events two arms combined, subsequent looks driven total number OS events two arms combined. Alternatively, analyses can planned occur specified calendar times.

title: Log-Rank Test Simulation for PFS and OS Endpoints — lrsim2e
code: lrsim2e(   kMax = 1,   kMaxpfs = 1,   hazardRatioH0pfs = 1,   hazardRatioH0os = 1,   allocation1 = 1,   allocation2 = 1,   accrualTime = 0,   accrualIntensity = NA,   piecewiseSurvivalTime = 0,   stratumFraction = 1,   rho_pd_os = 0,   lambda1pfs = NA,   lambda2pfs = NA,   lambda1os = NA,   lambda2os = NA,   gamma1pfs = 0,   gamma2pfs = 0,   gamma1os = 0,   gamma2os = 0,   n = NA,   followupTime = NA,   fixedFollowup = FALSE,   rho1 = 0,   rho2 = 0,   plannedEvents = NA,   plannedTime = NA,   maxNumberOfIterations = 1000,   maxNumberOfRawDatasetsPerStage = 0,   seed = 0,   nthreads = 0 )

title: Log-Rank Test Simulation for PFS and OS Endpoints — lrsim2e
text: kMax maximum number stages. kMaxpfs Number stages timing determined PFS events. Ranges 0 (none) kMax. hazardRatioH0pfs Hazard ratio null hypothesis active treatment vs control PFS. Defaults 1 superiority test. hazardRatioH0os Hazard ratio null hypothesis active treatment vs control OS. Defaults 1 superiority test. allocation1 Number subjects treatment group randomization block. Defaults 1 equal randomization. allocation2 Number subjects control group randomization block. Defaults 1 equal randomization. accrualTime vector specifies starting time piecewise Poisson enrollment time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 3) breaks time axis 2 accrual intervals: \([0, 3)\) \([3, \infty)\). accrualIntensity vector accrual intensities. One accrual time interval. piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 6) breaks time axis 2 event intervals: \([0, 6)\) \([6, \infty)\). Defaults 0 exponential distribution. stratumFraction vector stratum fractions sum 1. Defaults 1 stratification. rho_pd_os correlation coefficient standard bivariate normal random variables used generate time disease progression (PD) time death using inverse CDF method. lambda1pfs vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum treatment group PFS. lambda2pfs vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum control group PFS. lambda1os vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum treatment group OS. lambda2os vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum control group OS. gamma1pfs hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum treatment group PFS. gamma2pfs hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum control group PFS. gamma1os hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum treatment group OS. gamma2os hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum control group OS. n Sample size. followupTime Follow-time last enrolled subject. fixedFollowup Whether fixed follow-design used. Defaults FALSE variable follow-. rho1 first parameter Fleming-Harrington family weighted log-rank test. Defaults 0 conventional log-rank test. rho2 second parameter Fleming-Harrington family weighted log-rank test. Defaults 0 conventional log-rank test. plannedEvents planned cumulative total number PFS events Look 1 Look kMaxpfs planned cumulative total number OS events Look kMaxpfs+1 Look kMax. plannedTime calendar times analyses. use calendar time plan analyses, plannedEvents missing. maxNumberOfIterations number simulation iterations. Defaults 1000. maxNumberOfRawDatasetsPerStage number raw datasets per stage extract. seed seed reproduce simulation results. nthreads number threads use simulations (0 means default RcppParallel behavior).

title: Log-Rank Test Simulation for PFS and OS Endpoints — lrsim2e
text: list 2 components: sumdata: data frame summary data iteration stage: iterationNumber: iteration number. eventsNotAchieved: Whether target number events achieved iteration. stageNumber: stage number, covering stages even trial stops interim look. analysisTime: time stage since trial start. accruals1: number subjects enrolled stage treatment group. accruals2: number subjects enrolled stage control group. totalAccruals: total number subjects enrolled stage. endpoint: endpoint (1 PFS 2 OS) consideration. events1: number events stage treatment group. events2: number events stage control group. totalEvents: total number events stage. dropouts1: number dropouts stage treatment group. dropouts2: number dropouts stage control group. totalDropouts: total number dropouts stage. uscore: numerator log-rank test statistic endpoint. vscore: variance log-rank test statistic endpoint. logRankStatistic: log-rank test Z-statistic endpoint. rawdata (exists maxNumberOfRawDatasetsPerStage positive integer): data frame subject-level data selected replications, containing following variables: iterationNumber: iteration number. stageNumber: stage consideration. analysisTime: time stage since trial start. subjectId: subject ID. arrivalTime: enrollment time subject. stratum: stratum subject. treatmentGroup: treatment group (1 2) subject. endpoint: endpoint (1 PFS 2 OS) consideration row. subject two rows, one endpoint. survivalTime: underlying survival time event endpoint subject. dropoutTime: underlying dropout time event endpoint subject. timeUnderObservation: time observation since randomization event endpoint subject. event: Whether subject experienced event endpoint. dropoutEvent: Whether subject dropped endpoint.

what: Log-Rank Test Simulation for Two Endpoints (PFS and OS) and Three Arms — lrsim2e3a
title: Log-Rank Test Simulation for Two Endpoints (PFS and OS) and Three Arms — lrsim2e3a
text: Performs simulation two-endpoint (PFS OS) three-arm group sequential trials based weighted log-rank test. first kMaxpfs looks driven total number PFS events Arm Arm C combined, subsequent looks driven total number OS events Arm Arm C combined. Alternatively, analyses can planned occur specified calendar times.

title: Log-Rank Test Simulation for Two Endpoints (PFS and OS) and Three Arms — lrsim2e3a
text: kMax maximum number stages. kMaxpfs Number stages timing determined PFS events. Ranges 0 (none) kMax. hazardRatioH013pfs Hazard ratio null hypothesis arm 1 vs arm 3 PFS. Defaults 1 superiority test. hazardRatioH023pfs Hazard ratio null hypothesis arm 2 vs arm 3 PFS. Defaults 1 superiority test. hazardRatioH012pfs Hazard ratio null hypothesis arm 1 vs arm 2 PFS. Defaults 1 superiority test. hazardRatioH013os Hazard ratio null hypothesis arm 1 vs arm 3 OS. Defaults 1 superiority test. hazardRatioH023os Hazard ratio null hypothesis arm 2 vs arm 3 OS. Defaults 1 superiority test. hazardRatioH012os Hazard ratio null hypothesis arm 1 vs arm 2 OS. Defaults 1 superiority test. allocation1 Number subjects Arm randomization block. Defaults 1 equal randomization. allocation2 Number subjects Arm B randomization block. Defaults 1 equal randomization. allocation3 Number subjects Arm C randomization block. Defaults 1 equal randomization. accrualTime vector specifies starting time piecewise Poisson enrollment time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 3) breaks time axis 2 accrual intervals: \([0, 3)\) \([3, \infty)\). accrualIntensity vector accrual intensities. One accrual time interval. piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Defaults FALSE variable follow-. rho1 first parameter Fleming-Harrington family weighted log-rank test. Defaults 0 conventional log-rank test. rho2 second parameter Fleming-Harrington family weighted log-rank test. Defaults 0 conventional log-rank test. plannedEvents planned cumulative total number PFS events Look 1 Look kMaxpfs Arms C combined planned cumulative total number OS events Look kMaxpfs+1 Look kMax Arms C combined. plannedTime calendar times analyses. use calendar time plan analyses, plannedEvents missing. maxNumberOfIterations number simulation iterations. Defaults 1000. maxNumberOfRawDatasetsPerStage number raw datasets per stage extract. seed seed reproduce simulation results. nthreads number threads use simulations (0 means default RcppParallel behavior).

title: Log-Rank Test Simulation for Multi-Arm Multi-Stage Design — lrsim_mams
code: lrsim_mams(   M = 2,   kMax = 1,   criticalValues = NULL,   futilityBounds = NULL,   hazardRatioH0s = 1,   allocations = 1,   accrualTime = 0,   accrualIntensity = NA,   piecewiseSurvivalTime = 0,   stratumFraction = 1,   lambdas = NULL,   gammas = NULL,   n = NA,   followupTime = NA,   fixedFollowup = FALSE,   rho1 = 0,   rho2 = 0,   plannedEvents = NA,   plannedTime = NA,   maxNumberOfIterations = 1000,   maxNumberOfRawDatasetsPerStage = 0,   seed = 0,   nthreads = 0 )

title: Log-Rank Test Simulation for Multi-Arm Multi-Stage Design — lrsim_mams
text: M Number active treatment arms. kMax Number sequential looks. criticalValues matrix -level upper boundaries max z-test statistic scale efficacy stopping. first column level \(M\), second column level \(M - 1\), , last column level 1. Decision rule: Look 1, compute Wald statistic active arm versus common control. maximum statistics exceeds Look 1 critical value, stop efficacy check whether active arm can rejected using relaxed boundary closed testing principle. Look 1 stopping rule met, continue next look; maximum Wald statistics look exceeds corresponding level \(M\) critical value, stop efficacy; otherwise continue. critical value exceeded Look kMax, procedure ends without rejection. futilityBounds Numeric vector length kMax - 1 giving futility boundaries max-Z scale first kMax - 1 analyses. interim look, study stops futility active treatment arms cross futility boundary. final look, study counted stopping futility none active treatment arms can rejected. omitted, interim futility stopping applied. hazardRatioH0s Numeric vector length \(M\). Hazard ratios \(H_0\) active arm versus common control. Defaults 1 superiority tests. allocations Integer integer vector length \(M + 1\). Number subjects per arm within randomization block. single value implies equal allocation; defaults 1. accrualTime vector specifies starting time piecewise Poisson enrollment time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 3) breaks time axis 2 accrual intervals: \([0, 3)\) \([3, \infty)\). accrualIntensity vector accrual intensities. One accrual time interval. piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 6) breaks time axis 2 event intervals: \([0, 6)\) \([6, \infty)\). Defaults 0 exponential distribution. stratumFraction vector stratum fractions sum 1. Defaults 1 stratification. lambdas List length \(M\) (one element per arm). element scalar numeric vector event hazard rates corresponding arm, given analysis interval stratum required simulation. gammas List length \(M\) (one element per arm). element scalar numeric vector dropout hazard rates corresponding arm, analysis interval stratum. n Planned total sample size across active arms control. followupTime Follow-time last enrolled subject. fixedFollowup Whether fixed follow-design used. Defaults FALSE variable follow-. rho1 first parameter Fleming-Harrington family weighted log-rank test. Defaults 0 conventional log-rank test. rho2 second parameter Fleming-Harrington family weighted log-rank test. Defaults 0 conventional log-rank test. plannedEvents Numeric vector length \(K + 1\) giving planned cumulative number events trigger Look 1 Look \(K + 1\). entry refers combined events first active arm common control. Use plannedEvents schedule event-driven looks. plannedTime Numeric vector calendar times analyses. plannedTime supplied, analyses scheduled calendar time plannedEvents left missing. maxNumberOfIterations Number Monte Carlo replications. Defaults 1000. maxNumberOfRawDatasetsPerStage Number subject-level raw datasets retain per stage (selected replications). seed Random seed reproducibility. nthreads Number threads parallel simulation. Use 0 accept default RcppParallel behavior.

M: Number active arms Phase 2. K: Number sequential looks Phase 3. sumdata1: Data frame summarizing iteration, stage, treatment group: iterationNumber, eventsNotAchieved, stopStage, stageNumber, analysisTime, treatmentGroup, accruals, events, dropouts. stage final row summarizes overall study (arms combined). summdata2: Data frame summarizing log-rank statistics iteration, stage, active arm: iterationNumber, stopStage, stageNumber, analysisTime, activeArm, totalAccruals, totalEvents, totalDropouts, uscore, vscore, logRankStatistic, reject, futility. active arm, total accruals, events, dropouts refer combined counts arm common control stage. rawdata (present maxNumberOfRawDatasetsPerStage > 0): Subject-level data selected replications variables: iterationNumber, stopStage, stageNumber, analysisTime, subjectId, arrivalTime, stratum, treatmentGroup, survivalTime, dropoutTime, timeUnderObservation, event, dropoutEvent.

text: S3 object class "lrsim_seamless" components: overview: list summarizing trial-level results settings: selectAsBest: Probability selecting active arm best arm end phase 2. rejectPerStage: Probability rejecting null active arm stage. futilityPerStage: Probability futility stopping active arm stage. cumulativeRejection: Cumulative probability rejection stage. cumulativeFutility: Cumulative futility stopping probabilities stage. numberOfEvents: Cumulative event counts stage, including events arms stage 1 events selected arm control later stages. numberOfDropouts: Cumulative dropouts stage. numberOfSubjects: Cumulative enrollments stage. analysisTime: Average calendar time stage among replications reached stage. overallReject: Overall probability rejecting null trial end. overallFutility: Overall probability stopping futility trial end. expectedNumberOfEvents: Expected cumulative events trial end. expectedNumberOfDropouts: Expected cumulative dropouts trial end. expectedNumberOfSubjects: Expected cumulative enrollments trial end. expectedStudyDuration: Expected study duration. criticalValues: input critical values stage. futilityBounds: input futility boundaries stage. hazardRatioH0s: input hazard ratios \(H_0\). useEvents: Logical indicating whether analyses event-driven. numberOfIterations: Number simulation iterations performed. n: Planned total sample size. fixedFollowup: Logical indicating whether fixed follow-used. rho1, rho2: Fleming–Harrington weighting parameters used. M: Number active arms Phase 2. K: Number sequential looks Phase 3. sumdata1: Data frame summarizing iteration, stage, treatment group: iterationNumber, eventsNotAchieved, stopStage, stageNumber, analysisTime, treatmentGroup, accruals, events, dropouts. stage final row summarizes overall study (arms combined). summdata2: Data frame summarizing log-rank statistics iteration, stage, active arm: iterationNumber, bestArm, stopStage, stageNumber, analysisTime, activeArm, totalAccruals, totalEvents, totalDropouts, uscore, vscore, logRankStatistic, reject, futility. active arm, total accruals, events, dropouts refer combined counts arm common control stage. rawdata (present maxNumberOfRawDatasetsPerStage > 0): Subject-level data selected replications variables: iterationNumber, stopStage, stageNumber, analysisTime, subjectId, arrivalTime, stratum, treatmentGroup, survivalTime, dropoutTime, timeUnderObservation, event, dropoutEvent.

text: kMax maximum number stages. kMaxitt Number stages timing determined events ITT population. Ranges 0 (none) kMax. hazardRatioH0itt Hazard ratio null hypothesis ITT population. what: Number of Subjects Having an Event and Log-Rank Statistics — lrstat
title: Number of Subjects Having an Event and Log-Rank Statistics — lrstat
text: Obtains number subjects accrued, number events, number dropouts, number subjects reaching maximum follow-group, mean variance weighted log-rank score statistic, estimated hazard ratio weighted Cox regression variance log hazard ratio estimate given calendar times.

title: Number of Subjects Having an Event and Log-Rank Statistics — lrstat
code: lrstat(   time = NA_real_,   hazardRatioH0 = 1,   allocationRatioPlanned = 1,   accrualTime = 0L,   accrualIntensity = NA_real_,   piecewiseSurvivalTime = 0L,   stratumFraction = 1L,   lambda1 = NA_real_,   lambda2 = NA_real_,   gamma1 = 0L,   gamma2 = 0L,   accrualDuration = NA_real_,   followupTime = NA_real_,   fixedFollowup = FALSE,   rho1 = 0,   rho2 = 0,   predictTarget = 2L )

title: Number of Subjects Having an Event and Log-Rank Statistics — lrstat
text: time vector calendar times calculate number events mean variance log-rank test score statistic. hazardRatioH0 Hazard ratio null hypothesis active treatment versus control. Defaults 1 superiority test. allocationRatioPlanned Allocation ratio active treatment versus control. Defaults 1 equal randomization. accrualTime vector specifies starting time piecewise Poisson enrollment time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 3) breaks time axis 2 accrual intervals: \([0, 3)\) \([3, \infty)\). accrualIntensity vector accrual intensities. One accrual time interval. piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 6) breaks time axis 2 event intervals: \([0, 6)\) \([6, \infty)\). Defaults 0 exponential distribution. stratumFraction vector stratum fractions sum 1. Defaults 1 stratification. lambda1 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. lambda2 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum control group. gamma1 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. gamma2 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum control group. accrualDuration Duration enrollment period. followupTime Follow-time last enrolled subject. fixedFollowup Whether fixed follow-design used. Defaults FALSE variable follow-. rho1 first parameter Fleming-Harrington family weighted log-rank test. Defaults 0 conventional log-rank test. rho2 second parameter Fleming-Harrington family weighted log-rank test. Defaults 0 conventional log-rank test. predictTarget target prediction. Set predictTarget = 1 predict number events . Set predictTarget = 2 (default) predict number events log-rank score statistic mean variance. Set predictTarget = 3 predict number events, log-rank score statistic mean variance, hazard ratio variance log hazard ratio.

title: Number of Subjects Having an Event and Log-Rank Statistics — lrstat
text: data frame containing following variables predictTarget = 1: time: analysis time since trial start. subjects: number enrolled subjects. nevents: total number events. nevents1: number events active treatment group. nevents2: number events control group. ndropouts: total number dropouts. ndropouts1: number dropouts active treatment group. ndropouts2: number dropouts control group. nfmax: total number subjects reaching maximum follow-. nfmax1: number subjects reaching maximum follow-active treatment group. nfmax2: number subjects reaching maximum follow-control group. predictTarget = 2, following variables also included: uscore: numerator log-rank test statistic. vscore: variance log-rank score test statistic. logRankZ: log-rank test statistic Z-scale. hazardRatioH0: hazard ratio null hypothesis. Furthermore, predictTarget = 3, following additional variables also included: HR: average hazard ratio weighted Cox regression. vlogHR: variance log hazard ratio. zlogHR: Z-statistic log hazard ratio.

title: mTPI-2 Decision Table — mTPI2Table

title: mTPI-2 Decision Table — mTPI2Table

title: mTPI-2 Decision Table — mTPI2Table
text: nMax maximum number subjects dose cohort. pT target toxicity probability. Defaults 0.3. epsilon1 lower equivalence margin target. Defaults 0.05. epsilon2 upper equivalence margin target. Defaults 0.05. prior toxicity parameter beta prior. b prior non-toxicity parameter beta prior. pExcessTox threshold excessive toxicity, .e., Prob(p > pT | Data) > pExcessTox, current higher doses excluded never used remainder trial avoid subjects receiving treatment doses. Defaults 0.95.

title: mTPI-2 Decision Table — mTPI2Table
text: S3 class mTPI2Table object following components: settings: input settings data frame following variables: nMax: maximum number subjects dose cohort. pT: target toxicity probability. epsilon1: lower equivalence margin target. epsilon2: upper equivalence margin target. : prior toxicity parameter beta prior. b: prior non-toxicity parameter beta prior. pExcessTox: threshold excessive toxicity. subintervals: subintervals equal length mTPI-2 design. includes following variables: lower: lower bound subinterval. upper: upper bound subinterval. decision: dosing decision subinterval. decisionDataFrame: decision data frame mTPI-2 design. includes following variables: n: sample size. y: number toxicities. decision: dosing decision. decisionMatrix: decision matrix corresponding decision data frame.

title: mTPI-2 Decision Table — mTPI2Table

what: Number of Subjects at Risk — natrisk
title: Number of Subjects at Risk — natrisk
text: Obtains number subjects risk given analysis times treatment group.

title: Number of Subjects at Risk — natrisk
code: natrisk(   t = NA_real_,   allocationRatioPlanned = 1,   accrualTime = 0L,   accrualIntensity = NA_real_,   piecewiseSurvivalTime = 0L,   lambda1 = NA_real_,   lambda2 = NA_real_,   gamma1 = 0L,   gamma2 = 0L,   accrualDuration = NA_real_,   maxFollowupTime = NA_real_,   time = NA_real_ )

title: Number of Subjects at Risk — natrisk
text: t vector analysis times calculate number patients risk. allocationRatioPlanned Allocation ratio active treatment versus control. Defaults 1 equal randomization. accrualTime vector specifies starting time piecewise Poisson enrollment time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 3) breaks time axis 2 accrual intervals: \([0, 3)\) \([3, \infty)\). accrualIntensity vector accrual intensities. One accrual time interval. piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 6) breaks time axis 2 event intervals: \([0, 6)\) \([6, \infty)\). Defaults 0 exponential distribution. lambda1 vector hazard rates event active treatment group. One analysis time interval. lambda2 vector hazard rates event control group. One analysis time interval. gamma1 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout active treatment group. gamma2 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout control group. accrualDuration Duration enrollment period. maxFollowupTime Follow-time first enrolled subject. fixed follow-, maxFollowupTime = minFollowupTime. variable follow-, maxFollowupTime = accrualDuration + minFollowupTime. time Calendar time analysis.

title: Number of Subjects at Risk — natrisk
text: matrix number patients risk specified analysis times (row) treatment group (column).

title: Number of Subjects at Risk — natrisk
text: given treatment group \(g\) calendar time \(\tau\), number patients risk analysis time \(t\) calculated $$\phi_g (\tau - t) S_g(t) G_g(t),$$ \(\phi_g\) probability randomization treatment group \(g\), \((\tau - t)\) number patients enrolled calendar time \(\tau - t\), \(S_g(t)G_g(t)\) probability risk analysis time \(t\) patient treatment group \(g\) enrollment. Obviously, \(t < \min(\tau, T_{\rm{fmax}})\).

title: Number of Subjects at Risk — natrisk
text: Kaifeng Lu, kaifenglu@gmail.com

title: Number of Subjects at Risk — natrisk
code: # Piecewise accrual, piecewise exponential survivals, and 5% dropout by # the end of 1 year.  natrisk(t = c(9, 24), allocationRatioPlanned = 1,         accrualTime = c(0, 3), accrualIntensity = c(10, 20),         piecewiseSurvivalTime = c(0, 6),         lambda1 = c(0.0533, 0.0309), lambda2 = c(0.0533, 0.0533),         gamma1 = -log(1-0.05)/12, gamma2 = -log(1-0.05)/12,         accrualDuration = 12, maxFollowupTime = 30, time = 30) #>          [,1]     [,2] #> [1,] 66.88605 62.53900 #> [2,] 16.91289 11.30083

what: Power for Negative Binomial Rate Ratio — nbpower
title: Power for Negative Binomial Rate Ratio — nbpower
text: Estimates power negative binomial rate ratio test.

title: Power for Negative Binomial Rate Ratio — nbpower
code: nbpower(   kMax = 1L,   informationRates = NA_real_,   efficacyStopping = NA_integer_,   futilityStopping = NA_integer_,   criticalValues = NULL,   alpha = 0.025,   typeAlphaSpending = "sfOF",   parameterAlphaSpending = NA_real_,   userAlphaSpending = NA_real_,   futilityBounds = NULL,   futilityCP = NULL,   futilityRateRatio = NULL,   typeBetaSpending = "none",   parameterBetaSpending = NA_real_,   rateRatioH0 = 1,   allocationRatioPlanned = 1,   accrualTime = 0L,   accrualIntensity = NA_real_,   piecewiseSurvivalTime = 0L,   stratumFraction = 1L,   kappa1 = NA_real_,   kappa2 = NA_real_,   lambda1 = NA_real_,   lambda2 = NA_real_,   gamma1 = 0L,   gamma2 = 0L,   accrualDuration = NA_real_,   followupTime = NA_real_,   fixedFollowup = FALSE,   spendingTime = NA_real_,   studyDuration = NA_real_,   nullVariance = FALSE )

title: Power for Negative Binomial Rate Ratio — nbpower
text: kMax maximum number stages. informationRates information rates. Defaults (1:kMax) / kMax left unspecified. efficacyStopping Indicators whether efficacy stopping allowed stage. Defaults TRUE left unspecified. futilityStopping Indicators whether futility stopping allowed stage. Defaults TRUE left unspecified. criticalValues Upper boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping efficacy. alpha significance level. Defaults 0.025. typeAlphaSpending type alpha spending. One following: "" O'Brien-Fleming boundaries, "P" Pocock boundaries, "WT" Wang & Tsiatis boundaries, "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early efficacy stopping. Defaults "sfOF". parameterAlphaSpending parameter value alpha spending. Corresponds \(\Delta\) "WT", \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". userAlphaSpending user defined alpha spending. Cumulative alpha spent stage. futilityBounds Lower boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping futility stages 1, ..., kMax-1. Defaults rep(-8, kMax-1) left unspecified. futility bounds non-binding calculation critical values. futilityCP vector length kMax - 1 futility bounds conditional power scale. futilityRateRatio vector length kMax - 1 futility bounds rate ratio scale. typeBetaSpending type beta spending. One following: "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "none" early futility stopping. Defaults "none". parameterBetaSpending parameter value beta spending. Corresponds \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". rateRatioH0 Rate ratio null hypothesis. allocationRatioPlanned Allocation ratio active treatment versus control. Defaults 1 equal randomization. accrualTime vector specifies starting time piecewise Poisson enrollment time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 3) breaks time axis 2 accrual intervals: \([0, 3)\) \([3, \infty)\). accrualIntensity vector accrual intensities. One accrual time interval. piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 6) breaks time axis 2 event intervals: \([0, 6)\) \([6, \infty)\). Defaults 0 exponential distribution. stratumFraction vector stratum fractions sum 1. Defaults 1 stratification. kappa1 dispersion parameter (reciprocal shape parameter gamma mixing distribution) active treatment group stratum. kappa2 dispersion parameter (reciprocal shape parameter gamma mixing distribution) control group stratum. lambda1 rate parameter negative binomial distribution active treatment group stratum. lambda2 rate parameter negative binomial distribution control group stratum. gamma1 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. gamma2 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum control group. accrualDuration Duration enrollment period. followupTime Follow-time last enrolled subject. fixedFollowup Whether fixed follow-design used. Defaults FALSE variable follow-. spendingTime vector length kMax error spending time analysis. Defaults missing, case, informationRates. studyDuration Study duration fixed follow-design. Defaults missing, replaced sum accrualDuration followupTime. provided, value allowed less sum accrualDuration followupTime. nullVariance Whether calculate variance log rate ratio null hypothesis.

title: Power for Negative Binomial Rate Ratio — nbpower
text: S3 class nbpower object 4 components: overallResults: data frame containing following variables: overallReject: overall rejection probability. alpha: overall significance level. numberOfEvents: total number events. numberOfDropouts: total number dropouts. numbeOfSubjects: total number subjects. exposure: total exposure. studyDuration: total study duration. information: maximum information. expectedNumberOfEvents: expected number events. expectedNumberOfDropouts: expected number dropouts. expectedNumberOfSubjects: expected number subjects. expectedExposure: expected exposure. expectedStudyDuration: expected study duration. expectedInformation: expected information. accrualDuration: accrual duration. followupTime: follow-duration. fixedFollowup: Whether fixed follow-design used. kMax: number stages. rateRatioH0: rate ratio null hypothesis. rateRatio: rate ratio. byStageResults: data frame containing following variables: informationRates: information rates. efficacyBounds: efficacy boundaries Z-scale. futilityBounds: futility boundaries Z-scale. rejectPerStage: probability efficacy stopping. futilityPerStage: probability futility stopping. cumulativeRejection: cumulative probability efficacy stopping. cumulativeFutility: cumulative probability futility stopping. cumulativeAlphaSpent: cumulative alpha spent. numberOfEvents: number events. numberOfDropouts: number dropouts. numberOfSubjects: number subjects. exposure: exposure. analysisTime: average time since trial start. efficacyRateRatio: efficacy boundaries rate ratio scale. futilityRateRatio: futility boundaries rate ratio scale. efficacyP: efficacy boundaries p-value scale. futilityP: futility boundaries p-value scale. information: cumulative information. efficacyStopping: Whether allow efficacy stopping. futilityStopping: Whether allow futility stopping. settings: list containing following input parameters: typeAlphaSpending, parameterAlphaSpending, userAlphaSpending, typeBetaSpending, parameterBetaSpending, allocationRatioPlanned, accrualTime, accuralIntensity, piecewiseSurvivalTime, kappa1, kappa2, lambda1, lambda2, gamma1, gamma2, spendingTime, nullVariance. byTreatmentCounts: list containing following counts treatment group: numberOfEvents1: number events stage treatment group. numberOfDropouts1: number dropouts stage treatment group. numberOfSubjects1: number subjects stage treatment group. exposure1: exposure stage treatment group. numberOfEvents2: number events stage control group. numberOfDropouts2: number dropouts stage control group. numberOfSubjects2: number subjects stage control group. exposure2: exposure stage control group. expectedNumberOfEvents1: expected number events treatment group. expectedNumberOfDropouts1: expected number dropouts treatment group. expectedNumberOfSubjects1: expected number subjects treatment group. expectedExposure1: expected exposure treatment group. expectedNumberOfEvents2: expected number events control group. expectedNumberOfDropouts2: expected number dropouts control group. expectedNumberOfSubjects2: expected number subjects control group. expectedExposure2: expected exposure control group.

title: Power for Negative Binomial Rate Ratio — nbpower
text: Kaifeng Lu, kaifenglu@gmail.com

title: Power for Negative Binomial Rate Ratio — nbpower
code: # Example 1: Variable follow-up design  nbpower(kMax = 2, informationRates = c(0.5, 1),         alpha = 0.025, typeAlphaSpending = "sfOF",         accrualIntensity = 1956/1.25,         stratumFraction = c(0.2, 0.8),         kappa1 = 5, kappa2 = 5,         lambda1 = c(0.7*0.125, 0.75*0.25),         lambda2 = c(0.125, 0.25),         gamma1 = 0, gamma2 = 0,         accrualDuration = 1.25,         followupTime = 2.75, fixedFollowup = FALSE,         nullVariance = 1) #>                                                                              #> Group-sequential design with 2 stages for negative binomial rate ratio       #> Rate ratio under H0: 1, rate ratio under H1: 0.74                            #> Stratum fraction: 0.2 0.8                                                    #> Event rate for treatment: 0.0875 0.1875, event rate for control: 0.125 0.25  #> Dispersion for treatment: 5, dispersion for control: 5                       #> Overall power: 0.7316, overall significance level (1-sided): 0.025           #> Maximum # events: 1295.5, expected # events: 1163.1                          #> Maximum # dropouts: 0, expected # dropouts: 0                                #> Maximum # subjects: 1956, expected # subjects: 1956                          #> Maximum exposure: 6601.5, expected exposure: 5926.4                          #> Maximum information: 73.03, expected information: 68.23                      #> Total study duration: 4, expected study duration: 3.7                        #> Accrual duration: 1.2, follow-up duration: 2.8, fixed follow-up: FALSE       #> Allocation ratio: 1, variance of standardized test statistic: under H0       #> Alpha spending: Lan-DeMets O'Brien-Fleming, beta spending: None              #>                                                                              #>                                Stage 1 Stage 2 #> Information rate               0.500   1.000   #> Efficacy boundary (Z)          2.963   1.969   #> Cumulative rejection           0.1313  0.7316  #> Cumulative alpha spent         0.0015  0.0250  #> Number of events               286.5   1295.5  #> Number of dropouts             0.0     0.0     #> Number of subjects             1956.0  1956.0  #> Exposure                       1459.7  6601.5  #> Analysis time                  1.4     4.0     #> Efficacy boundary (rate ratio) 0.615   0.795   #> Efficacy boundary (p)          0.0015  0.0245  #> Information                    36.51   73.03    # Example 2: Fixed follow-up design  nbpower(kMax = 2, informationRates = c(0.5, 1),         alpha = 0.025, typeAlphaSpending = "sfOF",         accrualIntensity = 220/1.5,         kappa1 = 3, kappa2 = 3,         lambda1 = 0.5*8.4, lambda2 = 8.4,         gamma1 = 0, gamma2 = 0,         accrualDuration = 1.5,         followupTime = 0.5, fixedFollowup = TRUE) #>                                                                         #> Group-sequential design with 2 stages for negative binomial rate ratio  #> Rate ratio under H0: 1, rate ratio under H1: 0.5                        #> Event rate for treatment: 4.2, event rate for control: 8.4              #> Dispersion for treatment: 3, dispersion for control: 3                  #> Overall power: 0.7997, overall significance level (1-sided): 0.025      #> Maximum # events: 693, expected # events: 624                           #> Maximum # dropouts: 0, expected # dropouts: 0                           #> Maximum # subjects: 220, expected # subjects: 204.1                     #> Maximum exposure: 110, expected exposure: 99                            #> Maximum information: 16.38, expected information: 15.04                 #> Total study duration: 2, expected study duration: 1.8                   #> Accrual duration: 1.5, follow-up duration: 0.5, fixed follow-up: TRUE   #> Allocation ratio: 1, variance of standardized test statistic: under H1  #> Alpha spending: Lan-DeMets O'Brien-Fleming, beta spending: None         #>                                                                         #>                                Stage 1 Stage 2 #> Information rate               0.500   1.000   #> Efficacy boundary (Z)          2.963   1.969   #> Cumulative rejection           0.1639  0.7997  #> Cumulative alpha spent         0.0015  0.0250  #> Number of events               272.0   693.0   #> Number of dropouts             0.0     0.0     #> Number of subjects             123.0   220.0   #> Exposure                       43.2    110.0   #> Analysis time                  0.8     2.0     #> Efficacy boundary (rate ratio) 0.355   0.615   #> Efficacy boundary (p)          0.0015  0.0245  #> Information                    8.19    16.38

title: Sample Size for One-Sample Negative Binomial Rate — nbsamplesize1s
code: nbsamplesize1s(   beta = 0.2,   kMax = 1L,   informationRates = NA_real_,   efficacyStopping = NA_integer_,   futilityStopping = NA_integer_,   criticalValues = NULL,   alpha = 0.025,   typeAlphaSpending = "sfOF",   parameterAlphaSpending = NA_real_,   userAlphaSpending = NA_real_,   futilityBounds = NULL,   futilityCP = NULL,   futilityRate = NULL,   typeBetaSpending = "none",   parameterBetaSpending = NA_real_,   userBetaSpending = NA_real_,   lambdaH0 = NA_real_,   accrualTime = 0L,   accrualIntensity = NA_real_,   piecewiseSurvivalTime = 0L,   stratumFraction = 1L,   kappa = NA_real_,   lambda = NA_real_,   gamma = 0L,   accrualDuration = NA_real_,   followupTime = NA_real_,   fixedFollowup = FALSE,   spendingTime = NA_real_,   rounding = TRUE )

title: Sample Size for One-Sample Negative Binomial Rate — nbsamplesize1s
text: beta Type II error. Defaults 0.2. kMax maximum number stages. informationRates information rates. Defaults (1:kMax) / kMax left unspecified. efficacyStopping Indicators whether efficacy stopping allowed stage. Defaults TRUE left unspecified. futilityStopping Indicators whether futility stopping allowed stage. Defaults TRUE left unspecified. criticalValues Upper boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping efficacy. alpha significance level. Defaults 0.025. typeAlphaSpending type alpha spending. One following: "" O'Brien-Fleming boundaries, "P" Pocock boundaries, "WT" Wang & Tsiatis boundaries, "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early efficacy stopping. Defaults "sfOF". parameterAlphaSpending parameter value alpha spending. Corresponds \(\Delta\) "WT", \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". userAlphaSpending user defined alpha spending. Cumulative alpha spent stage. futilityBounds Lower boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping futility stages 1, ..., kMax-1. Defaults rep(-8, kMax-1) left unspecified. futility bounds non-binding calculation critical values. futilityCP vector length kMax - 1 futility bounds conditional power scale. futilityRate vector length kMax - 1 futility bounds rate scale. typeBetaSpending type beta spending. One following: "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early futility stopping. Defaults "none". parameterBetaSpending parameter value beta spending. Corresponds \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". userBetaSpending user defined beta spending. Cumulative beta spent stage. lambdaH0 rate parameter negative binomial distribution null hypothesis. accrualTime vector specifies starting time piecewise Poisson enrollment time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 3) breaks time axis 2 accrual intervals: \([0, 3)\) \([3, \infty)\). accrualIntensity vector accrual intensities. One accrual time interval. piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 6) breaks time axis 2 event intervals: \([0, 6)\) \([6, \infty)\). Defaults 0 exponential distribution. stratumFraction vector stratum fractions sum 1. Defaults 1 stratification. kappa dispersion parameter (reciprocal shape parameter gamma mixing distribution) negative binomial distribution stratum. lambda rate parameter negative binomial distribution alternative hypothesis stratum. gamma hazard rate exponential dropout vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout stratum. Defaults 0 dropout. accrualDuration Duration enrollment period. followupTime Follow-time last enrolled subject. fixedFollowup Whether fixed follow-design used. Defaults FALSE variable follow-. spendingTime vector length kMax error spending time analysis. Defaults missing, case, informationRates. rounding Whether round sample size. Defaults 1 sample size rounding.

title: Sample Size for Equivalence in Negative Binomial Rate Ratio — nbsamplesizeequiv
text: beta type II error. kMax maximum number stages. informationRates information rates. Defaults (1:kMax) / kMax left unspecified. criticalValues Upper boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping efficacy. alpha significance level two one-sided tests. Defaults 0.05. typeAlphaSpending type alpha spending. One following: "" O'Brien-Fleming boundaries, "P" Pocock boundaries, "WT" Wang & Tsiatis boundaries, "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early efficacy stopping. Defaults "sfOF". parameterAlphaSpending parameter value alpha spending. Corresponds \(\Delta\) "WT", \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". userAlphaSpending user defined alpha spending. Cumulative alpha spent stage. rateRatioLower lower equivalence limit rate ratio. rateRatioUpper upper equivalence limit rate ratio. allocationRatioPlanned Allocation ratio active treatment versus control. Defaults 1 equal randomization. accrualTime vector specifies starting time piecewise Poisson enrollment time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 3) breaks time axis 2 accrual intervals: \([0, 3)\) \([3, \infty)\). accrualIntensity vector accrual intensities. One accrual time interval. piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 6) breaks time axis 2 event intervals: \([0, 6)\) \([6, \infty)\). Defaults 0 exponential distribution. stratumFraction vector stratum fractions sum 1. Defaults 1 stratification. kappa1 dispersion parameter (reciprocal shape parameter gamma mixing distribution) active treatment group stratum. kappa2 dispersion parameter (reciprocal shape parameter gamma mixing distribution) control group stratum. lambda1 rate parameter negative binomial distribution active treatment group stratum. lambda2 rate parameter negative binomial distribution control group stratum. gamma1 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. gamma2 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum control group. accrualDuration Duration enrollment period. followupTime Follow-time last enrolled subject. fixedFollowup Whether fixed follow-design used. Defaults FALSE variable follow-. spendingTime vector length kMax error spending time analysis. Defaults missing, case, informationRates. rounding Whether round sample size. Defaults 1 sample size rounding.

title: Negative Binomial Rate Ratio — nbstat
text: Obtains number subjects accrued, number events, number dropouts, number subjects reaching maximum follow-, total exposure, variance log rate group, rate ratio, variance, Wald test statistic log rate ratio given calendar times.

title: Negative Binomial Rate Ratio — nbstat
code: nbstat(   time = NA_real_,   rateRatioH0 = 1,   allocationRatioPlanned = 1,   accrualTime = 0L,   accrualIntensity = NA_real_,   piecewiseSurvivalTime = 0L,   stratumFraction = 1L,   kappa1 = NA_real_,   kappa2 = NA_real_,   lambda1 = NA_real_,   lambda2 = NA_real_,   gamma1 = 0L,   gamma2 = 0L,   accrualDuration = NA_real_,   followupTime = NA_real_,   fixedFollowup = FALSE,   nullVariance = FALSE )

title: Negative Binomial Rate Ratio — nbstat
text: time vector calendar times data cut. rateRatioH0 Rate ratio null hypothesis. allocationRatioPlanned Allocation ratio active treatment versus control. Defaults 1 equal randomization. accrualTime vector specifies starting time piecewise Poisson enrollment time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 3) breaks time axis 2 accrual intervals: \([0, 3)\) \([3, \infty)\). accrualIntensity vector accrual intensities. One accrual time interval. piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 6) breaks time axis 2 event intervals: \([0, 6)\) \([6, \infty)\). Defaults 0 exponential distribution. stratumFraction vector stratum fractions sum 1. Defaults 1 stratification. kappa1 dispersion parameter (reciprocal shape parameter gamma mixing distribution) active treatment group stratum. kappa2 dispersion parameter (reciprocal shape parameter gamma mixing distribution) control group stratum. lambda1 rate parameter negative binomial distribution active treatment group stratum. lambda2 rate parameter negative binomial distribution control group stratum. gamma1 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. gamma2 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum control group. accrualDuration Duration enrollment period. followupTime Follow-time last enrolled subject. fixedFollowup Whether fixed follow-design used. Defaults FALSE variable follow-. nullVariance Whether calculate variance log rate ratio null hypothesis.

title: Negative Binomial Rate Ratio — nbstat
text: list two components: resultsUnderH1: data frame containing following variables: time: analysis time since trial start. subjects: number enrolled subjects. nevents: total number events. nevents1: number events active treatment group. nevents2: number events control group. ndropouts: total number dropouts. ndropouts1: number dropouts active treatment group. ndropouts2: number dropouts control group. nfmax: total number subjects reaching maximum follow-. nfmax1: number subjects reaching maximum follow-active treatment group. nfmax2: number subjects reaching maximum follow-control group. exposure: total exposure time. exposure1: exposure time active treatment group. exposure2: exposure time control group. rateRatio: rate ratio active treatment group versus control group. vlogRate1: variance log rate parameter active treatment group. vlogRate2: variance log rate parameter control group. vlogRR: variance log rate ratio. information: information log rate ratio. zlogRR: Z-statistic log rate ratio. resultsUnderH0 nullVariance = TRUE: data frame following variables: time: analysis time since trial start. lambda1H0: restricted maximum likelihood estimate event rate active treatment group. lambda2H0: restricted maximum likelihood estimate event rate control group. rateRatioH0: rate ratio H0. vlogRate1H0: variance log rate parameter active treatment group H0. vlogRate2H0: variance log rate parameter control group H0. vlogRRH0: variance log rate ratio H0. informationH0: information log rate ratio H0. zlogRRH0: Z-statistic log rate ratio variance evaluated H0. varianceRatio: ratio variance H0 versus variance H1. lambda1: true event rate active treatment group. lambda2: true event rate control group. rateRatio: true rate ratio. resultsUnderH0 nullVariance = FALSE: data frame following variables: time: analysis time since trial start. rateRatioH0: rate ratio H0. varianceRatio: Equal 1. lambda1: true event rate active treatment group. lambda2: true event rate control group. rateRatio: true rate ratio.

title: Negative Binomial Rate Ratio — nbstat
text: probability mass function negative binomial distribution dispersion parameter \(\kappa_i\) rate parameter \(\lambda_i\) given $$P(Y_{ij} = y) = \frac{\Gamma(y+1/\kappa_i)}{\Gamma(1/\kappa_i) y!} \left(\frac{1}{1 + \kappa_i \lambda_i t_{ij}}\right)^{1/\kappa_i} \left(\frac{\kappa_i \lambda_i t_{ij}} {1 + \kappa_i \lambda_i t_{ij}}\right)^{y},$$ \(Y_{ij}\) event count subject \(j\) treatment group \(\), \(t_{ij}\) exposure time subject. \(\kappa_i=0\), negative binomial distribution reduces Poisson distribution. treatment group \(\), let \(\beta_i = \log(\lambda_i)\). log-likelihood \(\{(\kappa_i, \beta_i):=1,2\}\) can written $$l = \sum_{=1}^{2}\sum_{j=1}^{n_{}} \{\log \Gamma(y_{ij} + 1/\kappa_i) - \log \Gamma(1/\kappa_i) + y_{ij} (\log(\kappa_i) + \beta_i) - (y_{ij} + 1/\kappa_i) \log(1+ \kappa_i \exp(\beta_i) t_{ij})\}.$$ follows $$\frac{\partial l}{\partial \beta_i} = \sum_{j=1}^{n_i} \left\{y_{ij} - (y_{ij} + 1/\kappa_i) \frac{\kappa_i \exp(\beta_i) t_{ij}} {1 + \kappa_i \exp(\beta_i)t_{ij}}\right\},$$ $$-\frac{\partial^2 l}{\partial \beta_i^2} = \sum_{j=1}^{n_i} (y_{ij} + 1/\kappa_i) \frac{\kappa_i \lambda_i t_{ij}} {(1 + \kappa_i \lambda_i t_{ij})^2}.$$ Fisher information \(\beta_i\) $$E\left(-\frac{\partial^2 l}{\partial \beta_i^2}\right) = n_i E\left(\frac{\lambda_i t_{ij}} {1 + \kappa_i \lambda_i t_{ij}}\right).$$ addition, can show $$E\left(-\frac{\partial^2 l} {\partial \beta_i \partial \kappa_i}\right) = 0.$$ Therefore, variance \(\hat{\beta}_i\) $$Var(\hat{\beta}_i) = \frac{1}{n_i} \left\{ E\left(\frac{\lambda_i t_{ij}}{1 + \kappa_i \lambda_i t_{ij}}\right) \right\}^{-1}.$$ evaluate integral, need obtain distribution exposure time, $$t_{ij} = \min(\tau - W_{ij}, C_{ij}, T_{fmax}),$$ \(\tau\) denotes calendar time since trial start, \(W_{ij}\) denotes enrollment time subject \(j\) treatment group \(\), \(C_{ij}\) denotes time dropout enrollment subject \(j\) treatment group \(\), \(T_{fmax}\) denotes maximum follow-time subjects. Therefore, $$P(t_{ij} \geq t) = P(W_{ij} \leq \tau - t)P(C_{ij} \geq t) (t\leq T_{fmax}).$$ Let \(H\) denote distribution function enrollment time, \(G_i\) denote survival function dropout time treatment group \(\). change variables, $$E\left(\frac{\lambda_i t_{ij}}{1 + \kappa_i \lambda_i t_{ij}} \right) = \int_{0}^{\tau \wedge T_{fmax}} \frac{\lambda_i}{(1 + \kappa_i \lambda_i t)^2} H(\tau - t) G_i(t) dt.$$ numerical integration algorithm univariate function can used evaluate integral. restricted maximum likelihood (reml) estimate \((\beta_1,\beta_2)\) subject constraint \(\beta_1 - \beta_2 = \Delta\), express log-likelihood terms \((\beta_2,\Delta,\kappa_1,\kappa_2)\), takes derivative log-likelihood function respect \(\beta_2\). resulting score equation asymptotic limit $$E\left(\frac{\partial l}{\partial \beta_2}\right) = s_1 + s_2,$$ $$s_1 = n r E\left\{\lambda_1 t_{1j} - \left(\lambda_1t_{1j} + \frac{1}{\kappa_1}\right) \frac{\kappa_1 e^{\tilde{\beta}_2 + \Delta}t_{1j}}{1 + \kappa_1 e^{\tilde{\beta}_2 +\Delta}t_{1j}}\right\},$$ $$s_2 = n (1-r) E\left\{\lambda_2 t_{2j} - \left(\lambda_2 t_{2j} + \frac{1}{\kappa_2}\right) \frac{\kappa_2 e^{\tilde{\beta}_2} t_{2j}} {1 + \kappa_2 e^{\tilde{\beta}_2}t_{2j}}\right\}.$$ \(r\) randomization probability active treatment group. asymptotic limit reml \(\beta_2\) solution \(\tilde{\beta}_2\) \(E\left(\frac{\partial l}{\partial \beta_2}\right) = 0.\)

title: Number of Subjects Having an Event by Calendar Time — nevent
text: time vector calendar times calculate number patients event. allocationRatioPlanned Allocation ratio active treatment versus control. Defaults 1 equal randomization. accrualTime vector specifies starting time piecewise Poisson enrollment time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 3) breaks time axis 2 accrual intervals: \([0, 3)\) \([3, \infty)\). accrualIntensity vector accrual intensities. One accrual time interval. piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 6) breaks time axis 2 event intervals: \([0, 6)\) \([6, \infty)\). Defaults 0 exponential distribution. lambda1 vector hazard rates event active treatment group. One analysis time interval. lambda2 vector hazard rates event control group. One analysis time interval. gamma1 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout active treatment group. gamma2 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout control group. accrualDuration Duration enrollment period. maxFollowupTime Follow-time first enrolled subject. fixed follow-, maxFollowupTime = minFollowupTime. variable follow-, maxFollowupTime = accrualDuration + minFollowupTime.

title: Number of Subjects Having an Event by Calendar Time — nevent
text: matrix number patients event specified calendar times (row) treatment group (column).

title: Number of Subjects Having an Event by Calendar Time — nevent
text: given treatment group \(g\) calendar time \(\tau\), number patients event calendar time \(\tau\) calculated \(I_1 + I_2\), $$I_1 = \phi_g (\tau - T_{\rm{fmax}}) P_g(T_{\rm{fmax}}),$$ $$I_2 = \phi_g \int_{\tau - T_{\rm{fmax}}}^{\tau} (u) P_g(\tau - u) du,$$ \(\phi_g\) probability randomization treatment group \(g\), \((\tau - T_{\rm{fmax}})\) number patients enrolled calendar time \(\tau - T_{\rm{fmax}}\), \(P_g(T_{\rm{fmax}})\) probability event maximum follow-time \(T_{\rm{fmax}}\) patient treatment group \(g\) enrollment, \((u)\) accrual intensity calendar time \(u\), \(P_g(\tau - u)\) probability event calendar time \(\tau\) patient treatment group \(g\) enrolled calendar time \(u\).
