title: Probability of Having an Event — pevent
code: # Piecewise exponential survival with hazard 0.0533 in the first 6 # months, and hazard 0.0309 thereafter, and 5% dropout by the end of # 1 year.  pevent(time = c(3, 9), piecewiseSurvivalTime = c(0, 6),        lambda = c(0.0533, 0.0309), gamma = -log(1-0.05)/12) #> [1] 0.1468527 0.3326891

title: Proportional Hazards Regression Models — phregr
text: Obtains hazard ratio estimates proportional hazards regression model right censored counting process data.

title: Proportional Hazards Regression Models — phregr
code: phregr(   data,   stratum = "",   time = "time",   time2 = "",   event = "event",   covariates = "",   weight = "",   offset = "",   id = "",   ties = "efron",   init = NA_real_,   robust = FALSE,   est_basehaz = TRUE,   est_resid = TRUE,   firth = FALSE,   plci = FALSE,   alpha = 0.05,   maxiter = 50,   eps = 1e-09 )

title: Proportional Hazards Regression Models — phregr
text: data input data frame contains following variables: stratum: stratum. time: follow-time right censored data, left end interval counting process data. time2: right end interval counting process data. Intervals assumed open left closed right, event indicates whether event occurred right end interval. event: event indicator, 1=event, 0=event. covariates: values baseline covariates (time-dependent covariates interval counting process data). weight: weight observation. offset: offset observation. id: optional subject ID counting process data time-dependent covariates. stratum name(s) stratum variable(s) input data. time name time variable left end interval counting process data input data. time2 name right end interval counting process data input data. event name event variable input data. covariates vector names baseline time-dependent covariates input data. weight name weight variable input data. offset name offset variable input data. id name id variable input data. ties method handling ties, either "breslow" "efron" (default). init vector initial values. Defaults zero variables. robust Whether robust sandwich variance estimate computed. presence id variable, score residuals aggregated id computing robust sandwich variance estimate. est_basehaz Whether estimate baseline hazards. Defaults TRUE. est_resid Whether estimate martingale residuals. Defaults TRUE. firth Whether use Firth’s penalized likelihood method. Defaults FALSE. plci Whether obtain profile likelihood confidence interval. alpha two-sided significance level. maxiter maximum number iterations. eps tolerance declare convergence.

title: Proportional Hazards Regression Models — phregr
text: list following components: sumstat: data frame summary statistics model fit following variables: n: number observations. nevents: number events. loglik0: (penalized) log-likelihood null. loglik1: maximum (penalized) log-likelihood. scoretest: score test statistic. niter: number Newton-Raphson iterations. ties: method handling ties, either "breslow" "efron". p: number columns Cox model design matrix. robust: Whether use robust variance estimate. firth: Whether use Firth's penalized likelihood method. fail: Whether model fails converge. loglik0_unpenalized: unpenalized log-likelihood null. loglik1_unpenalized: maximum unpenalized log-likelihood. parest: data frame parameter estimates following variables: param: name covariate parameter estimate. beta: log hazard ratio estimate. sebeta: standard error log hazard ratio estimate. z: Wald test statistic log hazard ratio. expbeta: hazard ratio estimate. lower: lower limit confidence interval. upper: upper limit confidence interval. p: p-value chi-square test. method: method compute confidence interval p-value. sebeta_naive: naive standard error log hazard ratio estimate robust variance requested. basehaz: data frame baseline hazards following variables (est_basehaz TRUE): time: observed event time. nrisk: number patients risk time point. nevent: number events time point. haz: baseline hazard time point. varhaz: variance baseline hazard time point assuming parameter beta known. gradhaz: gradient baseline hazard respect beta time point (presence covariates). stratum: stratum. residuals: martingale residuals. linear_predictors: vector linear predictors. p: number parameters. param: parameter names. beta: parameter estimate. vbeta: covariance matrix parameter estimates. vbeta_naive: naive covariance matrix parameter estimates. terms: terms object. xlevels: record levels factors used fitting. settings: list containing input parameter values.

title: Proportional Hazards Regression Models — phregr
text: Per K. Anderson Richard D. Gill. Cox's regression model counting processes, large sample study. Annals Statistics 1982; 10:1100-1120. Terry M. Therneau Patricia M. Grambsch. Modeling Survival Data: Extending Cox Model. Springer-Verlag, 2000.

title: Proportional Hazards Regression Models — phregr
text: Kaifeng Lu, kaifenglu@gmail.com

title: Proportional Hazards Regression Models — phregr
code: library(dplyr)  # Example 1 with right-censored data (fit1 <- phregr(   data = rawdata %>% filter(iterationNumber == 1) %>%     mutate(treat = 1*(treatmentGroup == 1)),   stratum = "stratum",   time = "timeUnderObservation", event = "event",   covariates = "treat", est_basehaz = FALSE, est_resid = FALSE)) #>     n nevents   loglik0   loglik1  lrchisq df    pvalue scoretest niter  ties #> 1 491     376 -1893.975 -1892.253 3.444581  1 0.0634595  3.449958     2 efron #>  #>   param       coef exp(coef)  se(coef)         z          p #> 1 treat -0.1920025 0.8253048 0.1035279 -1.854596 0.06365391  # Example 2 with counting process data and robust variance estimate (fit2 <- phregr(   data = heart %>% mutate(rx = as.numeric(transplant) - 1),   time = "start", time2 = "stop", event = "event",   covariates = c("rx", "age"), id = "id",   robust = TRUE, est_basehaz = TRUE, est_resid = TRUE)) #>     n nevents   loglik0   loglik1  lrchisq df     pvalue scoretest niter  ties #> 1 172      75 -298.1214 -295.5367 5.169366  2 0.07541998  4.641254     3 efron #>  #>   param         coef exp(coef)   se(coef)  robust se           z          p #> 1    rx -0.004178247 0.9958305 0.31208501 0.31415106 -0.01330012 0.98938835 #> 2   age  0.030742256 1.0312197 0.01449603 0.01518176  2.02494619 0.04287289

what: Multivariate Normal Probabilities over a Hyperrectangle — pmvnormr
title: Multivariate Normal Probabilities over a Hyperrectangle — pmvnormr
text: Computes probability multivariate normal random vector falls within rectangular region defined lower upper bounds.

title: Multivariate Normal Probabilities over a Hyperrectangle — pmvnormr
code: pmvnormr(   lower = NULL,   upper = NULL,   mean = NULL,   sigma,   n0 = 1024,   n_max = 16384,   R = 8,   abseps = 1e-04,   releps = 0,   seed = 0,   parallel = TRUE,   nthreads = 0 )

title: Multivariate Normal Probabilities over a Hyperrectangle — pmvnormr
text: lower numeric vector lower integration limits. upper numeric vector upper integration limits. mean mean vector. NULL (default), zero vector appropriate length used. sigma covariance (correlation) matrix distribution. n0 Initial number samples per replication Monte Carlo integration. n_max Maximum number samples allowed per replication. R Number independent replications used estimate error. abseps Absolute error tolerance probability calculation. releps Relative error tolerance probability calculation. seed Random seed reproducibility. 0, seed generated computer clock. parallel Logical; TRUE, computations performed parallel. nthreads Number threads parallel execution. 0, default RcppParallel behavior used.

title: Multivariate Normal Probabilities over a Hyperrectangle — pmvnormr
text: estimated probability following attributes: method: "exact" analytic methods "qmc" Monte Carlo approach. error: estimated error (half-width 95% confidence interval). nsamples: total number samples used across replications.

title: Multivariate Normal Probabilities over a Hyperrectangle — pmvnormr
text: function automatically selects efficient computation method based input: Analytic Methods: Used univariate cases multivariate distributions compound symmetry correlation structure non-negative correlations. Monte Carlo Estimation: Used cases. algorithm employs randomized quasi-Monte Carlo (QMC) approach using generalized Halton sequences. improve efficiency accuracy, QMC approach incorporates: Sequential Conditioning: Mimics standardization transformation approach used mvtnorm::lpmvnorm, reducing \(J\)-dimensional integral \((J-1)\)-dimensional problem hypercube. Adaptive Sampling: number samples per replication increases dynamically estimated error falls abseps releps, n_max reached. standard error derived \(R\) independent replications. high-dimensional problems, computations can accelerated setting parallel = TRUE, distributes replications across multiple CPU threads via nthreads.

title: Multivariate Normal Probabilities over a Hyperrectangle — pmvnormr
text: Kaifeng Lu, kaifenglu@gmail.com

title: Multivariate Normal Probabilities over a Hyperrectangle — pmvnormr
code: # Example 1: Compound symmetry covariance structure and analytic method n <- 5 mean <- rep(0, n) lower <- rep(-1, n) upper <- rep(3, n) sigma <- matrix(0.5, n, n) diag(sigma) <- 1 pmvnormr(lower, upper, mean, sigma) #> [1] 0.5800477 #> attr(,"method") #> [1] "analytic" #> attr(,"error") #> [1] 0 #> attr(,"nsamples") #> [1] 1  # Example 2: General covariance structure and Monte Carlo method n <- 5 mean <- rep(0, n) lower <- rep(-1, n) upper <- rep(3, n) sigma <- matrix(c(1, 0.5, 0.3, 0.2, 0.1,                   0.5, 1, 0.4, 0.3, 0.2,                   0.3, 0.4, 1, 0.5, 0.3,                   0.2, 0.3, 0.5, 1, 0.4,                   0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.4, 1), nrow = n) pmvnormr(lower, upper, mean, sigma, seed = 314159) #> [1] 0.5259248 #> attr(,"method") #> [1] "qmc" #> attr(,"error") #> [1] 9.473211e-05 #> attr(,"nsamples") #> [1] 16384

what: Distribution Function of Truncated Piecewise Exponential Distribution — ptpwexp
title: Distribution Function of Truncated Piecewise Exponential Distribution — ptpwexp
text: Obtains probability truncated piecewise exponential distribution.

title: Distribution Function of Truncated Piecewise Exponential Distribution — ptpwexp
code: ptpwexp(   q,   piecewiseSurvivalTime = 0,   lambda = 0.0578,   lowerBound = 0,   lower.tail = TRUE,   log.p = FALSE )

title: Distribution Function of Truncated Piecewise Exponential Distribution — ptpwexp
text: q vector quantiles. piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 6) breaks time axis 2 event intervals: \([0, 6)\) \([6, \infty)\). Defaults 0 exponential distribution. lambda vector hazard rates event. One analysis time interval. lowerBound left truncation time point survival time. Defaults 0 truncation. lower.tail Logical; TRUE (default), probabilities P(X <= x), otherwise, P(X > x). log.p Logical; TRUE, probabilities p given log(p).

title: Distribution Function of Truncated Piecewise Exponential Distribution — ptpwexp
text: probability p P(X > q | X > lowerBound) = 1 - p.

title: Distribution Function of Truncated Piecewise Exponential Distribution — ptpwexp
text: Kaifeng Lu, kaifenglu@gmail.com

title: Distribution Function of Truncated Piecewise Exponential Distribution — ptpwexp

what: Piecewise Exponential Approximation to a Survival Distribution — pwexpcuts
title: Piecewise Exponential Approximation to a Survival Distribution — pwexpcuts
text: Obtains piecewise exponential distribution approximates survival distribution.

title: Piecewise Exponential Approximation to a Survival Distribution — pwexpcuts
code: pwexpcuts(S, ..., tol = 1e-04)

title: Piecewise Exponential Approximation to a Survival Distribution — pwexpcuts
text: S survival function univariate survival time. ... Additional arguments passed S. tol tolerance convergence profile log-likelihood. Defaults 0.0001.

title: Piecewise Exponential Approximation to a Survival Distribution — pwexpcuts
text: list three components: piecewiseSurvivalTime: vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 6) breaks time axis 2 event intervals: [0, 6) [6, Inf). lambda: vector hazard rates event. One analysis time interval. loglik: sequence asymptotic limit piecewise exponential log-likelihood increasing number change points.

title: Piecewise Exponential Approximation to a Survival Distribution — pwexpcuts
text: function computes piecewise exponential approximation survival distribution. piecewise exponential model divides time axis \(J\) intervals defined change points, interval \([t_j, t_{j+1})\) constant hazard rate \(\lambda_j\). time intervals specified : $$[t_1, t_2), [t_2, t_3), \ldots, [t_{J}, t_{J+1})$$ \(t_1 = 0\), \(t_{J+1} = \infty\), \(t_j = \tau_{j-1}\) \(j = 2, \ldots, J\). function starts \(J = 2\) (1 change point) gradually increases \(J\) adding one change point time maximized profile log-likelihood change points stabilizes, .e., relative increase maximum profile log-likelihood function less tol. relative change hazard rate also less tol, function stops returns results.

title: Piecewise Exponential Approximation to a Survival Distribution — pwexpcuts
text: Kaifeng Lu, kaifenglu@gmail.com

title: Piecewise Exponential Approximation to a Survival Distribution — pwexpcuts

title: Profile Log-Likelihood Function for Change Points in Piecewise Exponential Approximation — pwexploglik
text: tau numeric vector change points. S survival function univariate survival time. ... Additional arguments passed S.

title: Profile Log-Likelihood Function for Change Points in Piecewise Exponential Approximation — pwexploglik
text: list following three components: piecewiseSurvivalTime: vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. lambda: vector hazard rates event. One analysis time interval. loglik: value profile log-likelihood.

title: Profile Log-Likelihood Function for Change Points in Piecewise Exponential Approximation — pwexploglik
text: function computes profile log-likelihood change points piecewise exponential survival model. Let \(S(t)\) denote survival function univariate survival time, \(\tau\) vector \(J-1\) change points. piecewise exponential survival model divides time axis \(J\) intervals defined change points \(\tau\), interval \([t_j, t_{j+1})\) constant hazard rate \(\lambda_j\). time intervals specified : $$[t_1, t_2), [t_2, t_3), \ldots, [t_{J}, t_{J+1})$$ \(t_1 = 0\), \(t_{J+1} = \infty\), \(t_j = \tau_{j-1}\) \(j = 2, \ldots, J\). subject, expected number events occurring \(j\)-th interval $$d_j = E\{(t_j < Y \leq t_{j+1})\} = S(t_j) - S(t_{j+1})$$ expected exposure \(j\)-th interval : $$e_j = E\{(Y-t_j)(t_j < Y \leq t_{j+1}) + (t_{j+1} - t_j)(Y > t_{j+1})\}$$ can shown equivalent $$e_j = \int_{t_j}^{t_{j+1}} S(t) dt$$ log-likelihood piecewise exponential model : $$\ell(\tau,\lambda) = \sum_{j=1}^J \{d_j \log(\lambda_j) - e_j \lambda_j\}$$ profile log-likelihood \(\tau\) obtained maximizing \(\ell(\tau,\lambda)\) respect \(\lambda\) fixed \(\tau\). maximum likelihood estimate hazard rate \(j\)-th interval $$\lambda_j = \frac{d_j}{e_j}$$ Substituting back, profile log-likelihood $$\ell(\tau) = \sum_{j=1}^J d_j \log(d_j/e_j) - 1$$ use fact \(\sum_{j=1}^J d_j = 1\).

title: Profile Log-Likelihood Function for Change Points in Piecewise Exponential Approximation — pwexploglik
text: Kaifeng Lu, kaifenglu@gmail.com

title: Profile Log-Likelihood Function for Change Points in Piecewise Exponential Approximation — pwexploglik

title: Repeated p-Values for Group Sequential Design — repeatedPValue
code: repeatedPValue(   kMax,   typeAlphaSpending = "sfOF",   parameterAlphaSpending = NA,   maxInformation = 1,   p,   information,   spendingTime = NULL,   nthreads = 0 )

title: Repeated p-Values for Group Sequential Design — repeatedPValue
text: kMax maximum number stages. typeAlphaSpending type alpha spending. One following: "" O'Brien-Fleming boundaries, "P" Pocock boundaries, "WT" Wang & Tsiatis boundaries, "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early efficacy stopping. Defaults "sfOF". parameterAlphaSpending parameter value alpha spending. Corresponds \(\Delta\) "WT", \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". maxInformation target maximum information. Defaults 1, case, information represents informationRates. p raw p-values look 1 look k. can matrix k columns k <= kMax. information observed information look. can matrix k columns. spendingTime error spending time analysis, must increasing less equal 1. Defaults NULL, case, informationRates derived information maxInformation. can matrix k columns. nthreads number threads use simulations (0 means default RcppParallel behavior).

title: Repeated p-Values for Group Sequential Design — repeatedPValue
text: repeated p-values look 1 look k.

title: Repeated p-Values for Group Sequential Design — repeatedPValue
text: Kaifeng Lu, kaifenglu@gmail.com

title: Repeated p-Values for Group Sequential Design — repeatedPValue
code: # Example 1: informationRates different from spendingTime repeatedPValue(kMax = 3, typeAlphaSpending = "sfOF",                maxInformation = 800,                p = c(0.2, 0.15, 0.1),                information = c(529, 700, 800),                spendingTime = c(0.6271186, 0.8305085, 1)) #> [1] 0.3101673 0.2258992 0.1232186  # Example 2: Maurer & Bretz (2013), current look is not the last look repeatedPValue(kMax = 3, typeAlphaSpending = "sfOF",                p = matrix(c(0.0062, 0.017,                             0.009, 0.13,                             0.0002, 0.0035,                             0.002, 0.06),                           nrow=4, ncol=2),                information = c(1/3, 2/3),                nthreads = 1) #>           [,1]        [,2] #> [1,] 0.1140577 0.002393359 #> [2,] 0.1682137 0.017160592 #> [3,] 0.1315366 0.011648306 #> [4,] 0.3820275 0.128460197

what: Residuals for Parametric Regression Models for Failure Time Data — residuals_liferegr
title: Residuals for Parametric Regression Models for Failure Time Data — residuals_liferegr
text: Obtains response, martingale, deviance, dfbeta, likelihood displacement residuals parametric regression model failure time data.

title: Residuals for Parametric Regression Models for Failure Time Data — residuals_liferegr
code: residuals_liferegr(   object,   type = c("response", "martingale", "deviance", "dfbeta", "dfbetas", "working",     "ldcase", "ldresp", "ldshape", "matrix"),   collapse = FALSE,   weighted = (type %in% c("dfbeta", "dfbetas")) )

title: Residuals for Parametric Regression Models for Failure Time Data — residuals_liferegr
text: object output phregr call. type type residuals desired, options including "response", "martingale", "deviance", "dfbeta", "dfbetas", "working", "ldcase", "ldresp", "ldshape", "matrix". collapse Whether collapse residuals id. weighted Whether compute weighted residuals.

title: Residuals for Parametric Regression Models for Failure Time Data — residuals_liferegr
text: Either vector matrix residuals, depending specified type: response residuals scale original data. martingale residuals event indicators minus cumulative hazards event right-censored data. working residuals scale linear predictor. deviance residuals log-likelihood scale. dfbeta residuals returned matrix, \(\)-th row represents approximate change model coefficients resulting inclusion subject \(\). dfbetas residuals similar dfbeta residuals, column scaled standard deviation corresponding coefficient. matrix residuals matrix derivatives log-likelihood function. Let \(L\) log-likelihood, \(p\) linear predictor (\(X\beta\)), \(s\) \(log(\sigma)\). resulting matrix contains six columns: \(L\), \(\partial L/\partial p\), \(\partial^2 L/\partial p^2\), \(\partial L/\partial s\), \(\partial^2 L/\partial s^2\), \(\partial L^2/\partial p\partial s\). ldcase residulas likelihood displacement case weight perturbation. ldresp residuals likelihood displacement response value perturbation. ldshape residuals likelihood displacement related shape parameter.

title: Residuals for Parametric Regression Models for Failure Time Data — residuals_liferegr
text: algorithms follow residuals.survreg function survival package, except martingale residuals, defined event right-censored data exponential, weibull, lognormal, loglogistic distributions.

title: Residuals for Parametric Regression Models for Failure Time Data — residuals_liferegr
text: Escobar, L. . Meeker, W. Q. Assessing influence regression analysis censored data. Biometrics 1992; 48:507-528.

title: Residuals for Parametric Regression Models for Failure Time Data — residuals_liferegr
text: Kaifeng Lu, kaifenglu@gmail.com

title: Residuals for Parametric Regression Models for Failure Time Data — residuals_liferegr
code: library(dplyr)  fit1 <- liferegr(   data = tobin %>% mutate(time = ifelse(durable>0, durable, NA)),   time = "time", time2 = "durable",   covariates = c("age", "quant"), dist = "normal")  resid <- residuals_liferegr(fit1, type = "response") head(resid) #> [1] 3.0496868 5.0125418 0.5416331 0.2560716 1.8501772 2.4098780

what: Residuals for Proportional Hazards Regression Models — residuals_phregr
title: Residuals for Proportional Hazards Regression Models — residuals_phregr
text: Obtains martingale, deviance, score, Schoenfeld residuals proportional hazards regression model.

title: Residuals for Proportional Hazards Regression Models — residuals_phregr
code: residuals_phregr(   object,   type = c("martingale", "deviance", "score", "schoenfeld", "dfbeta", "dfbetas",     "scaledsch"),   collapse = FALSE,   weighted = (type %in% c("dfbeta", "dfbetas")) )

title: Residuals for Proportional Hazards Regression Models — residuals_phregr
text: object output phregr call. type type residuals desired, options including "martingale", "deviance", "score", "schoenfeld", "dfbeta", "dfbetas", "scaledsch". collapse Whether collapse residuals id. applicable Schoenfeld type residuals. weighted Whether compute weighted residuals.

title: Residuals for Proportional Hazards Regression Models — residuals_phregr
text: martingale deviance residuals, result vector one element corresponding subject (without collapse). score residuals, result matrix row represents subject column corresponds variable. row order aligns input data used original fit. Schoenfeld residuals, result matrix one row event one column per variable. rows sorted time within strata, attributes stratum time included. Score residuals represent individual's contribution score vector. Two commonly used transformations dfbeta, represents approximate change coefficient vector observation excluded, dfbetas, gives approximate change coefficients scaled standard error coefficients.

title: Residuals for Proportional Hazards Regression Models — residuals_phregr
text: score Schoenfeld type residuals, proportional hazards model must include least one covariate. algorithms deviance, dfbeta, dfbetas, scaledsch residuals follow residuals.coxph function survival package.

title: Residuals for Proportional Hazards Regression Models — residuals_phregr
text: Terry M. Therneau, Patricia M. Grambsch, Thomas M. Fleming. Martingale based residuals survival models. Biometrika 1990; 77:147-160. Patricia M. Grambsch Terry M. Therneau. Proportional hazards tests diagnostics based weighted residuals. Biometrika 1994; 81:515-26.

title: Residuals for Proportional Hazards Regression Models — residuals_phregr
text: Kaifeng Lu, kaifenglu@gmail.com

title: Residuals for Proportional Hazards Regression Models — residuals_phregr
code: library(dplyr)  # Example 1 with right-censored data fit1 <- phregr(data = rawdata %>% filter(iterationNumber == 1) %>%                  mutate(treat = 1*(treatmentGroup == 1)),                stratum = "stratum",                time = "timeUnderObservation", event = "event",                covariates = "treat")  ressco <- residuals_phregr(fit1, type = "score") head(ressco) #> [1]  0.2344213 -0.2232656  0.1964093  0.5382246  0.3367144 -0.2268484  # Example 2 with counting process data fit2 <- phregr(data = heart %>% mutate(rx = as.numeric(transplant) - 1),                time = "start", time2 = "stop", event = "event",                covariates = c("rx", "age"), id = "id", robust = TRUE)  resssch <- residuals_phregr(fit2, type = "scaledsch") head(resssch) #>              rx         age #> [1,] -0.3762107  0.12783993 #> [2,]  0.1024506 -0.03363229 #> [3,] -0.4513918  0.11091644 #> [4,] -0.4282462  0.10487560 #> [5,] -0.8083496  0.14573692 #> [6,]  0.2879278 -0.14038327

text: data input data frame contains following variables: stratum: stratum. treat: treatment. time: possibly right-censored survival time. event: event indicator. stratum name stratum variable input data. treat name treatment variable input data. time name time variable input data. event name event variable input data. milestone milestone time calculate restricted mean survival time. rmstDiffH0 difference restricted mean survival times null hypothesis. Defaults 0.95. biascorrection Whether apply bias correction variance estimate. Defaults bias correction.

code: rmpower(   kMax = 1L,   informationRates = NA_real_,   efficacyStopping = NA_integer_,   futilityStopping = NA_integer_,   criticalValues = NULL,   alpha = 0.025,   typeAlphaSpending = "sfOF",   parameterAlphaSpending = NA_real_,   userAlphaSpending = NA_real_,   futilityBounds = NULL,   futilityCP = NULL,   futilityRmstDiff = NULL,   typeBetaSpending = "none",   parameterBetaSpending = NA_real_,   milestone = NA_real_,   rmstDiffH0 = 0,   allocationRatioPlanned = 1,   accrualTime = 0L,   accrualIntensity = NA_real_,   piecewiseSurvivalTime = 0L,   stratumFraction = 1L,   lambda1 = NA_real_,   lambda2 = NA_real_,   gamma1 = 0L,   gamma2 = 0L,   accrualDuration = NA_real_,   followupTime = NA_real_,   fixedFollowup = FALSE,   spendingTime = NA_real_,   studyDuration = NA_real_ )

title: Power for Difference in Restricted Mean Survival Times — rmpower
text: kMax maximum number stages. informationRates information rates. Defaults (1:kMax) / kMax left unspecified. efficacyStopping Indicators whether efficacy stopping allowed stage. Defaults TRUE left unspecified. futilityStopping Indicators whether futility stopping allowed stage. Defaults TRUE left unspecified. criticalValues Upper boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping efficacy. alpha significance level. Defaults 0.025. typeAlphaSpending type alpha spending. One following: "" O'Brien-Fleming boundaries, "P" Pocock boundaries, "WT" Wang & Tsiatis boundaries, "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early efficacy stopping. Defaults "sfOF". parameterAlphaSpending parameter value alpha spending. Corresponds \(\Delta\) "WT", \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". userAlphaSpending user defined alpha spending. Cumulative alpha spent stage. futilityBounds Lower boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping futility stages 1, ..., kMax-1. Defaults rep(-8, kMax-1) left unspecified. futility bounds non-binding calculation critical values. futilityCP vector length kMax - 1 futility bounds conditional power scale. futilityRmstDiff vector length kMax - 1 futility bounds restricted mean survival time difference scale. typeBetaSpending type beta spending. One following: "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "none" early futility stopping. Defaults "none". parameterBetaSpending parameter value beta spending. Corresponds \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". milestone milestone time calculate restricted mean survival time. rmstDiffH0 difference restricted mean survival times null hypothesis. Defaults 0 superiority test. allocationRatioPlanned Allocation ratio active treatment versus control. Defaults 1 equal randomization. accrualTime vector specifies starting time piecewise Poisson enrollment time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 3) breaks time axis 2 accrual intervals: \([0, 3)\) \([3, \infty)\). accrualIntensity vector accrual intensities. One accrual time interval. piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 6) breaks time axis 2 event intervals: \([0, 6)\) \([6, \infty)\). Defaults 0 exponential distribution. stratumFraction vector stratum fractions sum 1. Defaults 1 stratification. lambda1 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. lambda2 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum control group. gamma1 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. gamma2 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum control group. accrualDuration Duration enrollment period. followupTime Follow-time last enrolled subject. fixedFollowup Whether fixed follow-design used. Defaults FALSE variable follow-. spendingTime vector length kMax error spending time analysis. Defaults missing, case, informationRates. studyDuration Study duration fixed follow-design. Defaults missing, replaced sum accrualDuration followupTime. provided, value allowed less sum accrualDuration followupTime.

events: 356.4, expected # events: 333.2                                      #> Maximum # subjects: 468, expected # subjects: 468                                      #> Maximum information: 2.62, expected information: 2.53                                  #> Total study duration: 40, expected study duration: 37.2                                #> Accrual duration: 22, follow-up duration: 18, fixed follow-up: FALSE                   #> Allocation ratio: 1                                                                    #> Alpha spending: Lan-DeMets O'Brien-Fleming, beta spending: None                        #>                                                                                        #>                               Stage 1 Stage 2 #> Information rate              0.800   1.000   #> Efficacy boundary (Z)         2.250   2.025   #> Cumulative rejection          0.1739  0.3042  #> Cumulative alpha spent        0.0122  0.0250  #> Number of events              223.3   356.4   #> Number of dropouts            14.0    24.2    #> Number of subjects            468.0   468.0   #> Number of milestone subjects  17.2    140.9   #> Analysis time                 23.8    40.0    #> Efficacy boundary (rmst diff) 1.554   1.251   #> Efficacy boundary (p)         0.0122  0.0214  #> Information                   2.10    2.62

title: Sample Size for Difference in Restricted Mean Survival Times — rmsamplesize
text: beta Type II error. Defaults 0.2. kMax maximum number stages. informationRates information rates. Defaults (1:kMax) / kMax left unspecified. efficacyStopping Indicators whether efficacy stopping allowed stage. Defaults TRUE left unspecified. futilityStopping Indicators whether futility stopping allowed stage. Defaults TRUE left unspecified. criticalValues Upper boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping efficacy. alpha significance level. Defaults 0.025. typeAlphaSpending type alpha spending. One following: "" O'Brien-Fleming boundaries, "P" Pocock boundaries, "WT" Wang & Tsiatis boundaries, "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early efficacy stopping. Defaults "sfOF". parameterAlphaSpending parameter value alpha spending. Corresponds \(\Delta\) "WT", \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". userAlphaSpending user defined alpha spending. Cumulative alpha spent stage. futilityBounds Lower boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping futility stages 1, ..., kMax-1. Defaults rep(-8, kMax-1) left unspecified. futility bounds non-binding calculation critical values. futilityCP vector length kMax - 1 futility bounds conditional power scale. futilityRmstDiff vector length kMax - 1 futility bounds restricted mean survival time difference scale. typeBetaSpending type beta spending. One following: "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early futility stopping. Defaults "none". parameterBetaSpending parameter value beta spending. Corresponds \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". userBetaSpending user defined beta spending. Cumulative beta spent stage. milestone milestone time calculate restricted mean survival time. rmstDiffH0 difference restricted mean survival times null hypothesis. Defaults 0 superiority test. allocationRatioPlanned Allocation ratio active treatment versus control. Defaults 1 equal randomization. accrualTime vector specifies starting time piecewise Poisson enrollment time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 3) breaks time axis 2 accrual intervals: \([0, 3)\) \([3, \infty)\). accrualIntensity vector accrual intensities. One accrual time interval. piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 6) breaks time axis 2 event intervals: \([0, 6)\) \([6, \infty)\). Defaults 0 exponential distribution. stratumFraction vector stratum fractions sum 1. Defaults 1 stratification. lambda1 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. lambda2 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum control group. gamma1 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. gamma2 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum control group. accrualDuration Duration enrollment period. followupTime Follow-time last enrolled subject. fixedFollowup Whether fixed follow-design used. Defaults FALSE variable follow-. spendingTime vector length kMax error spending time analysis. Defaults missing, case, informationRates. rounding Whether round sample size. Defaults 1 sample size rounding.

Defaults TRUE left unspecified. futilityStopping Indicators whether futility stopping allowed stage. Defaults TRUE left unspecified. criticalValues Upper boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping efficacy. alpha significance level. Defaults 0.025. typeAlphaSpending type alpha spending. One following: "" O'Brien-Fleming boundaries, "P" Pocock boundaries, "WT" Wang & Tsiatis boundaries, "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early efficacy stopping. title: Sample Size for Equivalence in Restricted Mean Survival Time Difference — rmsamplesizeequiv
code: rmsamplesizeequiv(   beta = 0.2,   kMax = 1L,   informationRates = NA_real_,   criticalValues = NULL,   alpha = 0.05,   typeAlphaSpending = "sfOF",   parameterAlphaSpending = NA_real_,   userAlphaSpending = NA_real_,   milestone = NA_real_,   rmstDiffLower = NA_real_,   rmstDiffUpper = NA_real_,   allocationRatioPlanned = 1,   accrualTime = 0L,   accrualIntensity = NA_real_,   piecewiseSurvivalTime = 0L,   stratumFraction = 1L,   lambda1 = NA_real_,   lambda2 = NA_real_,   gamma1 = 0L,   gamma2 = 0L,   accrualDuration = NA_real_,   followupTime = NA_real_,   fixedFollowup = 0L,   spendingTime = NA_real_,   rounding = 1L )

title: Sample Size for Equivalence in Restricted Mean Survival Time Difference — rmsamplesizeequiv
text: beta type II error. kMax maximum number stages. informationRates information rates. Defaults (1:kMax) / kMax left unspecified. criticalValues Upper boundaries z-test statistic scale stopping efficacy. alpha significance level two one-sided tests. Defaults 0.05. typeAlphaSpending type alpha spending. One following: "" O'Brien-Fleming boundaries, "P" Pocock boundaries, "WT" Wang & Tsiatis boundaries, "sfOF" O'Brien-Fleming type spending function, "sfP" Pocock type spending function, "sfKD" Kim & DeMets spending function, "sfHSD" Hwang, Shi & DeCani spending function, "user" user defined spending, "none" early efficacy stopping. Defaults "sfOF". parameterAlphaSpending parameter value alpha spending. Corresponds \(\Delta\) "WT", \(\rho\) "sfKD", \(\gamma\) "sfHSD". userAlphaSpending user defined alpha spending. Cumulative alpha spent stage. milestone milestone time calculate restricted mean survival time. rmstDiffLower lower equivalence limit restricted mean survival time difference. rmstDiffUpper upper equivalence limit restricted mean survival time difference. allocationRatioPlanned Allocation ratio active treatment versus control. Defaults 1 equal randomization. accrualTime vector specifies starting time piecewise Poisson enrollment time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 3) breaks time axis 2 accrual intervals: \([0, 3)\) \([3, \infty)\). accrualIntensity vector accrual intensities. One accrual time interval. piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 6) breaks time axis 2 event intervals: \([0, 6)\) \([6, \infty)\). Defaults 0 exponential distribution. stratumFraction vector stratum fractions sum 1. Defaults 1 stratification. lambda1 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. lambda2 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum control group. gamma1 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. gamma2 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum control group. accrualDuration Duration enrollment period. followupTime Follow-time last enrolled subject. fixedFollowup Whether fixed follow-design used. Defaults FALSE variable follow-. spendingTime vector length kMax error spending time analysis. Defaults missing, case, informationRates. rounding Whether round sample size. Defaults 1 sample size rounding.

code: rmstat(   time = NA_real_,   milestone = NA_real_,   allocationRatioPlanned = 1,   accrualTime = 0L,   accrualIntensity = NA_real_,   piecewiseSurvivalTime = 0L,   stratumFraction = 1L,   lambda1 = NA_real_,   lambda2 = NA_real_,   gamma1 = 0L,   gamma2 = 0L,   accrualDuration = NA_real_,   followupTime = NA_real_,   fixedFollowup = FALSE )

title: Stratified Difference in Restricted Mean Survival Times — rmstat
text: time vector calendar times data cut. milestone milestone time calculate restricted mean survival time. allocationRatioPlanned Allocation ratio active treatment versus control. Defaults 1 equal randomization. accrualTime vector specifies starting time piecewise Poisson enrollment time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 3) breaks time axis 2 accrual intervals: \([0, 3)\) \([3, \infty)\). accrualIntensity vector accrual intensities. One accrual time interval. piecewiseSurvivalTime vector specifies starting time piecewise exponential survival time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 6) breaks time axis 2 event intervals: \([0, 6)\) \([6, \infty)\). Defaults 0 exponential distribution. stratumFraction vector stratum fractions sum 1. Defaults 1 stratification. lambda1 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. lambda2 vector hazard rates event analysis time interval stratum control group. gamma1 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum active treatment group. gamma2 hazard rate exponential dropout, vector hazard rates piecewise exponential dropout applicable strata, vector hazard rates dropout analysis time interval stratum control group. accrualDuration Duration enrollment period. followupTime Follow-time last enrolled subject. fixedFollowup Whether fixed follow-design used. Defaults FALSE variable follow-.

title: Stratified Difference in Restricted Mean Survival Times — rmstat
text: data frame containing following variables: time: calendar time since trial start. subjects: number enrolled subjects. nevents: total number events. nevents1: number events active treatment group. nevents2: number events control group. ndropouts: total number dropouts. ndropouts1: number dropouts active treatment group. ndropouts2: number dropouts control group. milestone: milestone time relative randomization. nmilestone: total number subjects reaching milestone. nmilestone1: number subjects reaching milestone active treatment group. nmiletone2: number subjects reaching milestone control group. rmst1: restricted mean survival time treatment group. rmst2: restricted mean survival time control group. rmstDiff: difference restricted mean survival times, .e., rmst1 - rmst2. vrmst1: variance rmst1. vrmst2: variance rmst2. vrmstDiff: variance rmstDiff. information: information rmstDiff, equal 1/vrmstDiff. rmstDiffZ: Z-statistic value, .e., rmstDiff/sqrt(vrmstDiff).

title: Stratified Difference in Restricted Mean Survival Times — rmstat
text: Kaifeng Lu, kaifenglu@gmail.com

title: Stratified Difference in Restricted Mean Survival Times — rmstat
code: # Piecewise accrual, piecewise exponential survivals, and 5% dropout by # the end of 1 year.  rmstat(time = c(22, 40),        milestone = 18,        allocationRatioPlanned = 1,        accrualTime = seq(0, 8),        accrualIntensity = 26/9*seq(1, 9),        piecewiseSurvivalTime = c(0, 6),        stratumFraction = c(0.2, 0.8),        lambda1 = c(0.0533, 0.0309, 1.5*0.0533, 1.5*0.0309),        lambda2 = c(0.0533, 0.0533, 1.5*0.0533, 1.5*0.0533),        gamma1 = -log(1-0.05)/12,        gamma2 = -log(1-0.05)/12,        accrualDuration = 22,        followupTime = 18, fixedFollowup = FALSE) #>   time subjects  nevents  nevents1 nevents2 ndropouts ndropouts1 ndropouts2 #> 1   22      468 195.2491  91.89764 103.3514  12.16925   6.202213   5.967036 #> 2   40      468 356.3965 164.83437 191.5622  24.15559  13.010003  11.145587 #>   milestone nmilestone nmilestone1 nmilestone2    rmst1    rmst2  rmstDiff #> 1        18   8.700526    5.138325    3.562201 10.85387 9.948097 0.9057767 #> 2        18 140.948521   83.240865   57.707656 10.85387 9.948097 0.9057767 #>      vrmst1    vrmst2 vrmstDiff information rmstDiffZ #> 1 0.2848075 0.2641123 0.5489198    1.821760  1.222550 #> 2 0.2029210 0.1785637 0.3814847    2.621337  1.466502

title: Simulation of Simon's Bayesian Basket Trials — simonBayesSim
text: Obtains simulated raw summary data Simon's Bayesian basket discovery trials.

title: Simulation of Simon's Bayesian Basket Trials — simonBayesSim
code: simonBayesSim(   p = NA_real_,   accrualTime = 0L,   accrualIntensity = NA_real_,   stratumFraction = 1L,   lambda = NA_real_,   gamma = NA_real_,   phi = NA_real_,   plo = NA_real_,   T = NA_real_,   maxSubjects = NA_integer_,   plannedSubjects = NA_integer_,   maxNumberOfIterations = 1000L,   maxNumberOfRawDatasets = 1L,   seed = 0L )

title: Simulation of Simon's Bayesian Basket Trials — simonBayesSim
text: p vector true response probabilities across strata. accrualTime vector specifies starting time piecewise Poisson enrollment time intervals. Must start 0, e.g., c(0, 3) breaks time axis 2 accrual intervals: \([0, 3)\) \([3, \infty)\). accrualIntensity vector accrual intensities. One accrual time interval. stratumFraction vector stratum fractions sum 1. Defaults 1 stratification. lambda prior probability drug activity homogeneous across strata. gamma prior probability drug active stratum. phi response probability active drug. plo response probability inactive drug. T threshold conclusive posterior probability stop enrollment. maxSubjects maximum total sample size. plannedSubjects planned cumulative number subjects stage. maxNumberOfIterations number simulation iterations. Defaults 1000. maxNumberOfRawDatasets number raw datasets extract. seed seed reproduce simulation results. seed environment used left unspecified,

title: Simulation of Simon's Bayesian Basket Trials — simonBayesSim
text: list containing following four components: rawdata: data frame subject-level data, containing following variables: iterationNumber: iteration number. stageNumber: stage number. subjectId: subject ID. arrivalTime: enrollment time subject. stratum: stratum subject. y: Whether subject responder (1) nonresponder (0). sumdata1: data frame simulation stratum-level summary data, containing following variables: iterationNumber: iteration number. stageNumber: stage number. stratum: stratum number. active: Whether drug active stratum. n: number subjects stratum. r: number responders stratum. posterior: posterior probability drug active stratum. open: Whether stratum still open enrollment. positive: Whether stratum determined positive stratum. negative: Whether stratum determined negative stratum. sumdata2: data frame simulation level summary data, containing following variables: iterationNumber: iteration number. numberOfStrata: total number strata. n_active_strata: number active strata. true_positive: number true positive strata. false_negative: number false negative strata. false_positive: number false positive strata. true_negative: number true negative strata. n_indet_strata: number indeterminate strata. numberOfSubjects: number subjects. overview: data frame summary across simulations, containing following variables: numberOfStrata: total number strata. n_active_strata: average number active strata. true_positive: average number true positive strata. false_negative: average number false negative strata. false_positive: average number false positive strata. true_negative: average number true negative strata. n_indet_strata: average number indeterminate strata. numberOfSubjects: average number subjects.

title: Simulation of Simon's Bayesian Basket Trials — simonBayesSim
text: Kaifeng Lu, kaifenglu@gmail.com

title: Simulation of Simon's Bayesian Basket Trials — simonBayesSim
code: sim1 <- simonBayesSim(   p = c(0.25, 0.25, 0.05),   accrualIntensity = 5,   stratumFraction = c(1/3, 1/3, 1/3),   lambda = 0.33, gamma = 0.5,   phi = 0.25, plo = 0.05,   T = 0.8, maxSubjects = 50,   plannedSubjects = seq(5, 50, 5),   maxNumberOfIterations = 1000,   maxNumberOfRawDatasets = 1,   seed = 314159)  sim1$overview #>   numberOfStrata n_active_strata true_positive false_negative false_positive #> 1              3               2         1.642          0.344          0.181 #>   true_negative n_indet_strata numberOfSubjects #> 1         0.805          0.028           27.015

title: lrstat 0.1.10
text: CRAN release: 2022-10-25 Add drift parameter getDesign function compute power given drift parameter. Update repeatedPValue function respect range repeated p-values allow matrix input raw p-values. Remove repeatedPValueFlag fseqbon function. Remove numSubintervals caltime function. Update description selected functions, parameters, output.

title: lrstat 0.1.9
text: CRAN release: 2022-10-09 Add fwgtmat fadjpsim calculate adjusted p-values Simes-based graphical approaches. update print method design, lrpower, lrsim.

title: lrstat 0.1.8
text: CRAN release: 2022-09-11 Add spendingTime getDesign, lrpower, lrsamplesize allow error spending time different information time. Rewrite lrsamplesize simplify accelerate computation typeOfComputation == “Schoenfeld”. Add getBound obtain efficacy stopping boundaries group sequential design allowing error spending time different information time. Add fadjpbon obtain adjusted p-values graphical approaches using weighted Bonferroni tests fixed design. Add updateGraph update weights transition matrix removing hypothesis set indices yet rejected null hypotheses. Add repeatedPValue Obtain repeated p-values group sequential design based given alpha spending function. Add fseqbon obtain test results group sequential trials using graphical approaches based weighted Bonferroni tests option provide repeated p-values hypothesis time. Add lrsim3a perform simulation three-arm group sequential trials based weighted log-rank test. looks driven total number events Arm Arm C combined. Add lrsim2e perform simulation two-endpoint two-arm group sequential trials based weighted log-rank test. first looks driven total number PFS events two arms combined, subsequent looks driven total number OS events two arms combined. Add lrsim2e3a perform simulation two-endpoint three-arm group sequential trials based weighted log-rank test. first looks driven total number PFS events Arm Arm C combined, subsequent looks driven total number OS events Arm Arm C combined.

Add number dropouts lrpower/lrsamplesize lrsim output. Add Schoenfeld method proportional hazards conventional log-rank test lrpower lrsamplesize.
